documents.title: Home
documents.bodyText: vanduo framework Essential just like water is. Ship pure HTML, CSS, and JavaScript straight to the browser. No npm install, no bundler, no build step — drop the CDN link in and you're productive in under a minute.. Every spacing unit, font-size step, and grid column is derived from the Golden Ratio and the Fibonacci sequence, so layouts feel balanced without manual tweaking.. Import the full vanduo.css or cherry-pick individual component files. Tree-shake what you don't need and keep your payload lean.. Built-in ARIA roles, visible focus rings, semantic HTML, keyboard-first interactions, and prefers-reduced-motion support — accessibility is on by default, not an add-on.. Light, dark, and system-synced themes with smooth CSS-variable transitions. Ships with a drop-in theme switcher and a live customizer.. A minimal, cache-friendly CSS footprint. Styling works with JavaScript disabled — scripts are progressive enhancements, never a requirement.. Readable, framework-free source. No JSX, no transpilation, no magic — just standards-compliant HTML, CSS, and vanilla JS you can audit line by line.. Every class is namespaced with the vd- prefix so Vanduo can safely coexist with Bootstrap, Tailwind, or your own legacy styles without cascade surprises.. Consistent naming, predictable DOM, fully documented components, and copy-paste examples. Easy to extend, theme, and contribute back to.. 1,500+ icons in 6 weights, bundled locally. No CDN required.. Regular. Fill. Bold. Light. Thin. Duotone. Icons by Phosphor Icons — MIT Licensed. See full documentation →. Vanduo is built on the Open Color system — clean, accessible hues with consistent 10-step scales.. Based on Open Color (MIT Licensed). See full palette in the documentation.. Explore all components, utilities, and code examples.

chunks.text: vanduo framework Essential just like water is
chunks.heading: vanduo framework

chunks.text: Ship pure HTML, CSS, and JavaScript straight to the browser. No npm install, no bundler, no build step — drop the CDN link in and you're productive in under a minute.
chunks.heading: Zero Dependencies

chunks.text: Every spacing unit, font-size step, and grid column is derived from the Golden Ratio and the Fibonacci sequence, so layouts feel balanced without manual tweaking.
chunks.heading: Fibonacci Design

chunks.text: Import the full vanduo.css or cherry-pick individual component files. Tree-shake what you don't need and keep your payload lean.
chunks.heading: Modular CSS

chunks.text: Built-in ARIA roles, visible focus rings, semantic HTML, keyboard-first interactions, and prefers-reduced-motion support — accessibility is on by default, not an add-on.
chunks.heading: Accessibility First

chunks.text: Light, dark, and system-synced themes with smooth CSS-variable transitions. Ships with a drop-in theme switcher and a live customizer.
chunks.heading: Dark Mode Ready

chunks.text: A minimal, cache-friendly CSS footprint. Styling works with JavaScript disabled — scripts are progressive enhancements, never a requirement.
chunks.heading: Lightweight & Fast

chunks.text: Readable, framework-free source. No JSX, no transpilation, no magic — just standards-compliant HTML, CSS, and vanilla JS you can audit line by line.
chunks.heading: Pure & Simple

chunks.text: Every class is namespaced with the vd- prefix so Vanduo can safely coexist with Bootstrap, Tailwind, or your own legacy styles without cascade surprises.
chunks.heading: Conflict-Free

chunks.text: Consistent naming, predictable DOM, fully documented components, and copy-paste examples. Easy to extend, theme, and contribute back to.
chunks.heading: Developer First

chunks.text: 1,500+ icons in 6 weights, bundled locally. No CDN required.
chunks.heading: Phosphor Icons

chunks.text: Icons by Phosphor Icons — MIT Licensed. See full documentation →
chunks.heading: Phosphor Icons

chunks.text: Vanduo is built on the Open Color system — clean, accessible hues with consistent 10-step scales.
chunks.heading: Open Color Palette

chunks.text: Based on Open Color (MIT Licensed). See full palette in the documentation.
chunks.heading: Open Color Palette

chunks.text: Explore all components, utilities, and code examples.
chunks.heading: Browse Docs

documents.title: About
headings: First, what Vanduo Framework is not
documents.bodyText: Mission, vision, and constraints that shape Vanduo Framework. A warm welcome and a message from Vanduo Foundation Open Source Board founding members. We invite you to explore our philosophy, and we genuinely appreciate the time you are taking to read our story.. The fact that you're here, spending a few of your minutes with us, means more than any star count or download metric ever could. So before anything else: thank you.. Let's clear the air.. Vanduo is not a competitor to Nuxt, Vue, or React. We are not here to replace your favorite tools, start a framework war, or convince you to rewrite your perfectly working application. Those projects are remarkable feats of engineering, maintained by brilliant people, and they solve real problems at enormous scale. We respect them deeply.. Vanduo does not operate on a Virtual DOM. There is no reconciliation algorithm running behind the scenes, no diffing tree, no synthetic event system. And that's by design — not by limitation.. Vanduo is the best friend of Dynamic Web, SPA, Lazy Loading, and Static Web alike.. Think of it this way: not every page on the internet needs a reactive state machine. Not every portfolio, documentation site, or landing page needs to ship a JavaScript runtime to the browser before a single word of text appears on screen. But those pages do deserve to appear beautiful, feel responsive, and load instantly.. That's where Vanduo lives.. We built Vanduo for the projects that come before and around the complex ones — the spaces where HTML, CSS, and a sprinkle of vanilla JavaScript are not just enough, but are the right tool for the job. Vanduo gives those projects a design system that would make any SPA jealous, without asking you to install a single dependency.. We started Vanduo because we noticed something: the web had become heavy. Not heavy in a bad-faith way — heavy because, somewhere along the line, the simplest possible website began requiring a node_modules folder, a bundler config, and a PhD in toolchain orchestration.. We asked ourselves: what if we went the other way?. What if we stripped away everything that doesn't serve the developer or the end user? What if we treated constraints — zero third-party dependencies, no mandatory build step, simple setup — not as limitations, but as a creative compass?. The answer was water. Essential, transparent, adaptable. Vanduo.. We hope to keep Vanduo lean . Every kilobyte should earn its place. If something doesn't serve you or your users, we want the courage to leave it out.. We dream of keeping Vanduo honest . No magic, no black boxes. You can read every line we ship — and we hope you will. Understanding your tools shouldn't require a decoder ring.. We aspire to keep Vanduo open — not open as a marketing strategy, but open as a principle. This is not a product with a free tier. This is a gift to the community, maintained with care, documented with pride, and released under a license that means what it says.. And above all, our deepest ambition is to keep Vanduo for you . Not for hype cycles, not for benchmarks, not for conference talks — for the developer sitting at a desk at 2 AM who just wants their site to look great without fighting a framework.. We're not there yet on all of these. But having the right ambitions matters, and these are ours.. The way people build for the web is changing — fast.. There are vibe coders spinning up entire projects in an afternoon with AI at the wheel. There are weekend builders turning side ideas into real products between Saturday coffee and Sunday dinner. There are solo devs wearing every hat, small teams punching above their weight, and hustlers who need their landing page live yesterday .. We see all of you. And we built Vanduo with all of you in mind.. Our ambition is to make Vanduo AI-friendly while being developer-friendly first — and we mean every kind of developer:. Vanduo works for all three because there is nothing hidden. No abstraction layer to confuse a language model. No magic syntax that only makes sense if you've read 400 pages of documentation. Just HTML, CSS, and JavaScript — the languages that both humans and AI already speak fluently.. That's also why we've already started including llms.txt files in our repository — structured, AI-readable summaries of the entire framework. It's a small step, but it's a natural evolution of how we build the web. Because if the future of development is a conversation between human creativity and machine capability, we want Vanduo to be a language they both understand.. We don't need to be the biggest framework. We don't need to be on every trending list. If Vanduo helps even one developer ship a project they're proud of — a project that loads in a blink, looks polished on every device, and was fun to build — then everything we've poured into this was worth it.. Water doesn't compete with fire. It doesn't compete with earth. It just flows, adapts, and gives life wherever it goes. Just as water is essential for life, we believe a good CSS framework should be essential yet unobtrusive -- flowing naturally into your projects without forcing you to change your workflow. Water adapts to its container; Vanduo adapts to your design.. That's the shape of the water. That's Vanduo.. We invite you to let your ideas flow just as freely. Join us, and let's build something lasting together.. With gratitude and warm regards,. Vanduo (pronounced van-doo-oh ) is derived from Lithuanian : vanduo means "water.". The full AI vibe coder who wants to describe a page and watch it materialize.. The hybrid developer who pairs with AI but keeps their hands on the wheel.. The no-AI purist who wants to understand every single line and wouldn't have it any other way.

chunks.text: Mission, vision, and constraints that shape Vanduo Framework
chunks.heading: About

chunks.text: A warm welcome and a message from Vanduo Foundation Open Source Board founding members
chunks.heading: The Shape of the Water

chunks.text: We invite you to explore our philosophy, and we genuinely appreciate the time you are taking to read our story.
chunks.heading: The Shape of the Water

chunks.text: The fact that you're here, spending a few of your minutes with us, means more than any star count or download metric ever could. So before anything else: thank you.
chunks.heading: The Shape of the Water

chunks.text: Let's clear the air.
chunks.heading: First, what Vanduo Framework is not

chunks.text: Vanduo is not a competitor to Nuxt, Vue, or React. We are not here to replace your favorite tools, start a framework war, or convince you to rewrite your perfectly working application. Those projects are remarkable feats of engineering, maintained by brilliant people, and they solve real problems at enormous scale. We respect them deeply.
chunks.heading: First, what Vanduo Framework is not

chunks.text: Vanduo does not operate on a Virtual DOM. There is no reconciliation algorithm running behind the scenes, no diffing tree, no synthetic event system. And that's by design — not by limitation.
chunks.heading: First, what Vanduo Framework is not

chunks.text: Vanduo is the best friend of Dynamic Web, SPA, Lazy Loading, and Static Web alike.
chunks.heading: So what is Vanduo?

chunks.text: Think of it this way: not every page on the internet needs a reactive state machine. Not every portfolio, documentation site, or landing page needs to ship a JavaScript runtime to the browser before a single word of text appears on screen. But those pages do deserve to appear beautiful, feel responsive, and load instantly.
chunks.heading: So what is Vanduo?

chunks.text: That's where Vanduo lives.
chunks.heading: So what is Vanduo?

chunks.text: We built Vanduo for the projects that come before and around the complex ones — the spaces where HTML, CSS, and a sprinkle of vanilla JavaScript are not just enough, but are the right tool for the job. Vanduo gives those projects a design system that would make any SPA jealous, without asking you to install a single dependency.
chunks.heading: So what is Vanduo?

chunks.text: We started Vanduo because we noticed something: the web had become heavy. Not heavy in a bad-faith way — heavy because, somewhere along the line, the simplest possible website began requiring a node_modules folder, a bundler config, and a PhD in toolchain orchestration.
chunks.heading: Why we built this

chunks.text: We asked ourselves: what if we went the other way?
chunks.heading: Why we built this

chunks.text: What if we stripped away everything that doesn't serve the developer or the end user? What if we treated constraints — zero third-party dependencies, no mandatory build step, simple setup — not as limitations, but as a creative compass?
chunks.heading: Why we built this

chunks.text: The answer was water. Essential, transparent, adaptable. Vanduo.
chunks.heading: Why we built this

chunks.text: We hope to keep Vanduo lean . Every kilobyte should earn its place. If something doesn't serve you or your users, we want the courage to leave it out.
chunks.heading: Our hopes, dreams, and ambitions

chunks.text: We dream of keeping Vanduo honest . No magic, no black boxes. You can read every line we ship — and we hope you will. Understanding your tools shouldn't require a decoder ring.
chunks.heading: Our hopes, dreams, and ambitions

chunks.text: We aspire to keep Vanduo open — not open as a marketing strategy, but open as a principle. This is not a product with a free tier. This is a gift to the community, maintained with care, documented with pride, and released under a license that means what it says.
chunks.heading: Our hopes, dreams, and ambitions

chunks.text: And above all, our deepest ambition is to keep Vanduo for you . Not for hype cycles, not for benchmarks, not for conference talks — for the developer sitting at a desk at 2 AM who just wants their site to look great without fighting a framework.
chunks.heading: Our hopes, dreams, and ambitions

chunks.text: We're not there yet on all of these. But having the right ambitions matters, and these are ours.
chunks.heading: Our hopes, dreams, and ambitions

chunks.text: The way people build for the web is changing — fast.
chunks.heading: For every kind of builder

chunks.text: There are vibe coders spinning up entire projects in an afternoon with AI at the wheel. There are weekend builders turning side ideas into real products between Saturday coffee and Sunday dinner. There are solo devs wearing every hat, small teams punching above their weight, and hustlers who need their landing page live yesterday .
chunks.heading: For every kind of builder

chunks.text: We see all of you. And we built Vanduo with all of you in mind.
chunks.heading: For every kind of builder

chunks.text: Our ambition is to make Vanduo AI-friendly while being developer-friendly first — and we mean every kind of developer:
chunks.heading: For every kind of builder

chunks.text: Vanduo works for all three because there is nothing hidden. No abstraction layer to confuse a language model. No magic syntax that only makes sense if you've read 400 pages of documentation. Just HTML, CSS, and JavaScript — the languages that both humans and AI already speak fluently.
chunks.heading: For every kind of builder

chunks.text: That's also why we've already started including llms.txt files in our repository — structured, AI-readable summaries of the entire framework. It's a small step, but it's a natural evolution of how we build the web. Because if the future of development is a conversation between human creativity and machine capability, we want Vanduo to be a language they both understand.
chunks.heading: For every kind of builder

chunks.text: The full AI vibe coder who wants to describe a page and watch it materialize.
chunks.heading: For every kind of builder

chunks.text: The hybrid developer who pairs with AI but keeps their hands on the wheel.
chunks.heading: For every kind of builder

chunks.text: The no-AI purist who wants to understand every single line and wouldn't have it any other way.
chunks.heading: For every kind of builder

chunks.text: We don't need to be the biggest framework. We don't need to be on every trending list. If Vanduo helps even one developer ship a project they're proud of — a project that loads in a blink, looks polished on every device, and was fun to build — then everything we've poured into this was worth it.
chunks.heading: A small thing can matter

chunks.text: Water doesn't compete with fire. It doesn't compete with earth. It just flows, adapts, and gives life wherever it goes. Just as water is essential for life, we believe a good CSS framework should be essential yet unobtrusive -- flowing naturally into your projects without forcing you to change your workflow. Water adapts to its container; Vanduo adapts to your design.
chunks.heading: A small thing can matter

chunks.text: That's the shape of the water. That's Vanduo.
chunks.heading: A small thing can matter

chunks.text: We invite you to let your ideas flow just as freely. Join us, and let's build something lasting together.
chunks.heading: A small thing can matter

chunks.text: With gratitude and warm regards,

documents.title: Changelog
documents.bodyText: All notable changes to Vanduo Framework and Vanduo Docs are documented here — side by side.. Horizontal flex strip that expands one panel at a time ( .vd-expanding-cards / .vd-expanding-card ), adapted from the MIT-licensed Expanding flex cards CodePen.. Removed the completion scale bounce ( .morph-done / vd-morph-bounce ) so the wave transition alone finishes the swap—easier on the eyes when reading.. Elevated cards use a stronger shadow token (and a subtle hover lift on non-link cards) so they read clearly against outlined and filled variants.. Format tokens YYYY / MM / DD now work case-insensitively (fixes values sticking as the literal mask). Initial value is parsed using the same format; min/max use local-midnight YYYY-MM-DD parsing; opening syncs to the selected month; calendar uses role="grid" , column headers, aria-disabled on blocked days, and keyboard navigation (arrows, Home/End, Page Up/Down, Enter/Space, Escape). Closing after pick or Escape no longer reopens the popup when focus returns to the input.. Spotlight tooltip, nav buttons, and highlight target use global radius tokens ( --card-border-radius , --btn-border-radius-sm , and --spotlight-target-radius , defaulting to --btn-border-radius ).. Optional scroll-reveal modifier with staggered delays and VanduoTimeline ( IntersectionObserver ; respects prefers-reduced-motion ).. Full section with image strip + solid-color demos, attribution to the original CodePen, and class reference.. Caption reveal spacing; larger status badges; Priority and Build Status morph-badge examples; manual badge demos no longer add a bounce class.. Clearer variant comparisons, dedicated Glow / Glass / Morph demos, compact & spacious sizing, expanded class reference.. Clearer copy for pre-selected/custom format, month/year/decade navigation, and min/max demos; API table notes case-insensitive format tokens and grid roles; accessibility list matches the component.. Site metadata, bundled docs/dist framework assets, and jsDelivr examples now reference v1.3.8 .. Demos use .vd-timeline-animated with scroll-into-view reveal; API table and alerts document the modifier and --vd-timeline-reveal-delay .. Framework bundle remains pinned at v1.3.5 . Docs-only release focused on navigation speed and feel.. Section HTML is now cached in-memory (LRU + sessionStorage ), prefetched on hover/touch and idle, served by a stale-while-revalidate service worker at sw.js , and swapped via document.startViewTransition when supported.. Search or deep-linking to bottom-of-sidebar sections ( Glass , Morph , Ripple …) now uses an instant scrollIntoView that bypasses the global scroll-behavior: smooth , so concurrent layout shifts from sibling Vanduo.init() can no longer cancel the scroll mid-path and the URL is no longer hijacked by scroll-spy before landing.. Sidebar / mobile TOC taps now immediately show the skeleton scroll loader and an .is-navigating pressed state, so there's no unresponsive gap during the fetch.. preloadSectionsBefore now fetches up to 6 preceding sections in parallel, and handleRoute threads an AbortController so rapid clicks cancel stale fetches instead of racing to the wrong section.. Sections use content-visibility: auto with contain-intrinsic-size , and #dynamic-content enables overflow-anchor to stabilize scroll during parallel inserts.. The docs sidebar surface now follows the theme, removing the harsh contrast edge in dark mode.. The nine feature cards on the home page now reveal their details via the Water Morph wave, and hover adds a subtle card-glow accent.. Docs-site ThemeCustomizer.DEFAULTS now use slate neutral and 0.5 radius.. Framework CDN pin remains at @v1.3.5 .. A liquid wave content-swap effect. Add .vd-morph or data-vd-morph to any element, provide .vd-morph-current / .vd-morph-next content layers, and clicking triggers a radial wave that dissolves the old state and reveals the new one. Size variants ( .vd-morph-sm , .vd-morph-lg ), custom --morph-wave-color , and a programmatic VanduoMorph.morph(el) API are included. Respects prefers-reduced-motion .. Default tooltips ( tooltips.css ) now use a light surface on light UI and a dark surface on dark UI, instead of always using a high-contrast inverted chip. Explicit vd-tooltip-light / vd-tooltip-dark variants remain available.. Chromium Tablet and WebKit Tablet projects in playwright.config.ts now set browserName explicitly ( chromium / webkit ) so tablet runs match their names and CI results stay reliable.. The GitHub Actions tablet workflow job timeout was raised from 20 to 45 minutes so full tablet suites can finish without spurious cancellations on slower runners.. A dedicated Water Morph page under Effects with four interactive demos: Mode Toggle, Theme Preview Card, Status Badge cycle, and Caption Reveal. Each demo has collapsible code snippets and the section includes full API reference tables and accessibility notes.. The sidebar toggle button now uses the framework .vd-morph classes instead of duplicated CSS keyframes, reducing docs-specific CSS by ~260 lines while keeping the same liquid wave animation.. The glass documentation page ( docs/sections/effects/glass.html ) now includes a “Why Distinctions Can Look Subtle” explainer, higher-frequency comparison backdrops, side-by-side baseline vs glass sample rows for all size variants and modifiers, and interactive diagnostics controls for backdrop mode plus forced floating hover preview.. Playwright specs for home page views and search ( page-views.spec.ts , search.spec.ts ) now assert the global search modal and trigger flow instead of removed hero inline search inputs, keeping CI aligned with the current site.. The labs repo adds Playwright ( playwright.config.ts ), a static harness page, and tests for utils/hex-math.js and VdHexGrid (interaction, terrain, pathfinding, theme observer) so vd-hex behavior is covered in the browser.. Introduced a complete frosted-glass surface system built on backdrop-filter . Core class .vd-glass provides translucent background, specular highlight sheen, noise texture, and box shadow. Size variants .vd-glass-sm / md / lg / xl tune blur and opacity. Modifiers .vd-glass-tinted , .vd-glass-floating , and .vd-glass-contrast cover the most common use patterns. Full prefers-reduced-transparency and @supports not (backdrop-filter) fallbacks included.. Added --glass-blur , --glass-saturate , --glass-bg-opacity , --glass-bg-light / dark , --glass-border-light / dark , --glass-highlight-alpha , --glass-shadow , and --glass-noise-opacity with automatic light and dark-mode overrides via [data-theme="dark"] and @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) . All tokens are aliased as --vd-glass-* in vd-aliases.css .. Opt-in .vd-*-glass modifier added to Navbar, Modal, Dropdown, Tooltip, Toast, Card, and FAB components, enabling frosted glass on any of them with a single class.. Navbar gains scroll-aware glass activation: transparent at rest, frosted on scroll. Threshold configurable via data-scroll-threshold . A generic data-glass-scroll + data-glass-sentinel attribute API (powered by IntersectionObserver in the new glass.js component) brings the same pattern to any element. The inactive state [data-glass-scroll]:not(.is-glass-active) is CSS-only; the JS toggles .is-glass-active .. The labs VdHexGrid now re-syncs selectedHex references after grid regeneration, preventing stale selection coordinates during rotation, size updates, and dimension changes.. Added docs/sections/effects/glass.html with a hero demo, token reference table, size variant showcase, modifier demos, component integration examples, a live scroll-activated glass demo using the data-glass-scroll pattern, and accessibility notes. Registered under the Effects category in sections.json .. Applied glass effects across the docs UI: scroll-activated frosted navbar ( .vd-navbar-glass ), frosted global search modal, glass feature cards and showcase cards on the home page, glass entry tiles and status/version cards on t

chunks.text: Horizontal flex strip that expands one panel at a time ( .vd-expanding-cards / .vd-expanding-card ), adapted from the MIT-licensed Expanding flex cards CodePen.
chunks.heading: Framework

chunks.text: Removed the completion scale bounce ( .morph-done / vd-morph-bounce ) so the wave transition alone finishes the swap—easier on the eyes when reading.
chunks.heading: Framework

chunks.text: Elevated cards use a stronger shadow token (and a subtle hover lift on non-link cards) so they read clearly against outlined and filled variants.
chunks.heading: Framework

chunks.text: Format tokens YYYY / MM / DD now work case-insensitively (fixes values sticking as the literal mask). Initial value is parsed using the same format; min/max use local-midnight YYYY-MM-DD parsing; opening syncs to the selected month; calendar uses role="grid" , column headers, aria-disabled on blocked days, and keyboard navigation (arrows, Home/End, Page Up/Down, Enter/Space, Escape). Closing after pick or Escape no longer reopens the popup when focus returns to the input.

chunks.text: A liquid wave content-swap effect. Add .vd-morph or data-vd-morph to any element, provide .vd-morph-current / .vd-morph-next content layers, and clicking triggers a radial wave that dissolves the old state and reveals the new one. Size variants ( .vd-morph-sm , .vd-morph-lg ), custom --morph-wave-color , and a programmatic VanduoMorph.morph(el) API are included. Respects prefers-reduced-motion .
chunks.heading: Framework

chunks.text: Default tooltips ( tooltips.css ) now use a light surface on light UI and a dark surface on dark UI, instead of always using a high-contrast inverted chip. Explicit vd-tooltip-light / vd-tooltip-dark variants remain available.
chunks.heading: Framework

chunks.text: Introduced a complete frosted-glass surface system built on backdrop-filter . Core class .vd-glass provides translucent background, specular highlight sheen, noise texture, and box shadow. Size variants .vd-glass-sm / md / lg / xl tune blur and opacity. Modifiers .vd-glass-tinted , .vd-glass-floating , and .vd-glass-contrast cover the most common use patterns. Full prefers-reduced-transparency and @supports not (backdrop-filter) fallbacks included.
chunks.heading: Framework

chunks.text: Added --glass-blur , --glass-saturate , --glass-bg-opacity , --glass-bg-light / dark , --glass-border-light / dark , --glass-highlight-alpha , --glass-shadow , and --glass-noise-opacity with automatic light and dark-mode overrides via [data-theme="dark"] and @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) . All tokens are aliased as --vd-glass-* in vd-aliases.css .
chunks.heading: Framework

chunks.text: Opt-in .vd-*-glass modifier added to Navbar, Modal, Dropdown, Tooltip, Toast, Card, and FAB components, enabling frosted glass on any of them with a single class.
chunks.heading: Framework

chunks.text: Navbar gains scroll-aware glass activation: transparent at rest, frosted on scroll. Threshold configurable via data-scroll-threshold . A generic data-glass-scroll + data-glass-sentinel attribute API (powered by IntersectionObserver in the new glass.js component) brings the same pattern to any element. The inactive state [data-glass-scroll]:not(.is-glass-active) is CSS-only; the JS toggles .is-glass-active .
chunks.heading: Framework

chunks.text: The labs VdHexGrid now re-syncs selectedHex references after grid regeneration, preventing stale selection coordinates during rotation, size updates, and dimension changes.

chunks.text: renderItems() now escapes text through _escapeHtml() before applying the highlight regex via innerHTML . Previously, raw server/user data was inserted directly — a malicious suggestion could execute arbitrary scripts.
chunks.heading: Framework

chunks.text: Selector .vd-custom-select-option corrected to .custom-select-option in updateSelectedOptions() , openDropdown() , and handleKeydown() . Keyboard navigation and programmatic updates were silently failing.
chunks.heading: Framework

chunks.text: generateId() assigns the generated ID to element.id before returning. Previously ARIA aria-labelledby pointed to a nonexistent ID, breaking screen-reader label association.
chunks.heading: Framework

chunks.text: The codesnippet:copy custom event now passes the real copySuccess result instead of a hardcoded true .
chunks.heading: Framework

chunks.text: A single shared keydown handler on document replaces per-modal listeners. Handler is installed on first modal init and removed by destroyAll() .
chunks.heading: Framework

chunks.text: _typeaheadBuffer and _typeaheadTimer are now stored per-instance in the instances Map. Previously, typing in one dropdown corrupted the typeahead state of another.
chunks.heading: Framework

chunks.text: Replaced document.body.style.overflow = 'hidden' with the CSS class .body-navbar-open . Prevents the navbar and modal scroll locks from clobbering each other.
chunks.heading: Framework

chunks.text: on() now returns the bound wrapper function so callers can pass it to off() to remove delegation listeners.
chunks.heading: Framework

chunks.text: User-supplied regex patterns in the pattern rule are now capped at 100 characters to prevent catastrophic backtracking.
chunks.heading: Framework

chunks.text: The match rule now applies CSS.escape() to the param before building the querySelector, preventing selector injection. Wrapped in try/catch for invalid selectors.
chunks.heading: Framework

chunks.text: Replaced this.img.onload = ... with addEventListener('load', ..., { once: true }) . Handler is cleaned up in close() if the image hasn't loaded yet.
chunks.heading: Framework

chunks.text: The duplicate this.applyTheme(this.DEFAULTS.THEME) call in reset() has been removed.
chunks.heading: Framework

chunks.text: Suggest — XSS in renderItems() renderItems() now escapes text through _escapeHtml() before applying the highlight regex via innerHTML . Previously, raw server/user data was inserted directly — a malicious suggestion could execute arbitrary scripts.
chunks.heading: Framework

chunks.text: Select — 3 broken querySelector selectors fixed Selector .vd-custom-select-option corrected to .custom-select-option in updateSelectedOptions() , openDropdown() , and handleKeydown() . Keyboard navigation and programmatic updates were silently failing.
chunks.heading: Framework

chunks.text: Select — generateId() now assigns element.id generateId() assigns the generated ID to element.id before returning. Previously ARIA aria-labelledby pointed to a nonexistent ID, breaking screen-reader label association.
chunks.heading: Framework

chunks.text: Code Snippet — codesnippet:copy event reports actual success The codesnippet:copy custom event now passes the real copySuccess result instead of a hardcoded true .
chunks.heading: Framework

chunks.text: Modals — single shared ESC listener replaces O(n) per-modal handlers A single shared keydown handler on document replaces per-modal listeners. Handler is installed on first modal init and removed by destroyAll() .
chunks.heading: Framework

chunks.text: Dropdown + Select — typeahead buffer moved to per-instance state _typeaheadBuffer and _typeaheadTimer are now stored per-instance in the instances Map. Previously, typing in one dropdown corrupted the typeahead state of another.
chunks.heading: Framework

chunks.text: Navbar — CSS class body-navbar-open replaces inline overflow:hidden Replaced document.body.style.overflow = 'hidden' with the CSS class .body-navbar-open . Prevents the navbar and modal scroll locks from clobbering each other.
chunks.heading: Framework

chunks.text: Helpers — on() delegation returns the wrapper for off() on() now returns the bound wrapper function so callers can pass it to off() to remove delegation listeners.
chunks.heading: Framework

chunks.text: Validate — 100-char limit on user regex patterns (ReDoS prevention) User-supplied regex patterns in the pattern rule are now capped at 100 characters to prevent catastrophic backtracking.
chunks.heading: Framework

chunks.text: Validate — match rule uses CSS.escape() on param The match rule now applies CSS.escape() to the param before building the querySelector, preventing selector injection. Wrapped in try/catch for invalid selectors.
chunks.heading: Framework

chunks.text: Image Box — addEventListener('load', {once:true}) replaces DOM0 onload Replaced this.img.onload = ... with addEventListener('load', ..., { once: true }) . Handler is cleaned up in close() if the image hasn't loaded yet.
chunks.heading: Framework

chunks.text: Theme Customizer — removed redundant second applyTheme() in reset() The duplicate this.applyTheme(this.DEFAULTS.THEME) call in reset() has been removed.
chunks.heading: Framework

chunks.text: Largest component release to date. Adds Carousel/Flow, Popover/Bubble, Scrollspy/Waypoint, Ripple/Waves, FAB, Sticky/Affix, Autocomplete/Suggest, Form Validation, Date Picker, Time Picker, Stepper, Timeline, Rating, Transfer/Multi-select, Tree View, and Spotlight/Feature Discovery. Each includes dedicated CSS, JavaScript, Playwright tests, and HTML fixtures.
chunks.heading: Framework

chunks.text: The existing Sidenav component now supports top, right, bottom, and left positioning via data-vd-position . Use the .vd-offcanvas alias class for a semantic offcanvas panel.
chunks.heading: Framework

chunks.text: Many new components use a Vanduo-themed primary name alongside a universally recognized alias (e.g., .vd-flow / .vd-carousel , .vd-bubble / .vd-popover , .vd-waypoint / .vd-scrollspy ). Both class names work interchangeably.
chunks.heading: Framework

chunks.text: Added 260 new utility classes for applying background and text colors from any Open Color hue at any shade level. Use vd-bg-{color}-{shade} for backgrounds and vd-text-{color}-{shade} for text colors. Covers all 13 hues (red, orange, yellow, lime, green, teal, cyan, blue, indigo, violet, grape, pink, gray) across shades 0–9.
chunks.heading: Framework

chunks.text: New: Per-Hue Color Utility Classes Added 260 new utility classes for applying background and text colors from any Open Color hue at any shade level. Use vd-bg-{color}-{shade} for backgrounds and vd-text-{color}-{shade} for text colors. Covers all 13 hues (red, orange, yellow, lime, green, teal, cyan, blue, indigo, violet, grape, pink, gray) across shades 0–9.

chunks.text: The Quick Start section was instructing users to import '@vanduo-oss/framework/dist/vanduo.min.css' . This subpath is not exposed by the package exports map, causing bundlers (Vite, Webpack 5, Rollup) to throw a subpath-not-defined error. Corrected to '@vanduo-oss/framework/css' — the proper exports-map entry.
chunks.heading: Documentation

chunks.text: The 7-step Getting Started guide contained Bootstrap-style navbar patterns that do not exist in Vanduo. vd-navbar-toggler → vd-navbar-toggle , data-toggle="collapse" and data-target="#mainNav" attributes removed, vd-navbar-collapse → vd-navbar-menu aria-hidden="true" , and vd-container inside nav → vd-navbar-container . Fixed in both the Step 3 snippet and the Complete File reference.
chunks.heading: Documentation

chunks.text: The "Package Manager" card (formerly first with a Recommended badge) has been renamed to "With a Bundler (Vite)" and moved to the last position. CDN is now first and Recommended — the correct default for users who just want to drop Vanduo into a page. The bundler card now includes a warning that bare module specifiers require a build tool, and a pnpm create vite scaffold step before the import snippet.
chunks.heading: Documentation

chunks.text: Fix: Incorrect CSS import path in Quick Start The Quick Start section was instructing users to import '@vanduo-oss/framework/dist/vanduo.min.css' . Corrected to '@vanduo-oss/framework/css' — the proper exports-map entry.

chunks.text: Touch event listeners for touchmove and touchend now use { passive: false } so preventDefault() works on modern browsers. Additionally, all preventDefault() calls are guarded with e.cancelable checks. Fixes the [Intervention] console warnings and enables proper drag operations on touch devices.
chunks.heading: Framework

chunks.text: Fix: Draggable Touch on Mobile Touch event listeners for touchmove and touchend now use { passive: false } so preventDefault() works on modern browsers. Additionally, all preventDefault() calls are guarded with e.cancelable checks. Fixes the [Intervention] console warnings and enables proper drag operations on touch devices.

chunks.text: When theme mode was set to "System", toggling the OS between dark and light mode changed backgrounds and text but left the primary accent color unchanged. Added a prefers-color-scheme media-query change listener in ThemeCustomizer that auto-switches the primary color between black (light) and amber (dark) when the user hasn't explicitly chosen a non-default color.

documents.title: Labs
documents.bodyText: Experimental features and demos. These are upcoming features that are not yet part of the framework and are subject to change.. Full source download and ES modules for vd-hex live at github.com/vanduo-oss/labs. Experimental interactive hex-grid toolkit for visualization and game prototypes. Source: vanduo-oss/labs. An experimental interactive hex-grid toolkit with selection, pan/zoom, terrain helpers, and optional rotation. Click a hex to inspect it and use the controls below to explore the API.. Standalone modules: github.com/vanduo-oss/labs .. Minimal markup for the canvas container plus JavaScript to create the same interactive grid as above—expand to copy or compare with your own project.. // Import the VdHexGrid component import { VdHexGrid } from './hex-grid.js'; // Initialize the hex grid const grid = new VdHexGrid({ element: document.getElementById('hex-demo-container'), canvas: document.getElementById('hex-demo'), size: 30, // Hex size in pixels width: 15, // Number of columns height: 10, // Number of rows rotation: 0 // Optional rotation in radians }); // Listen for hex selection grid.on('select', (hex) => { console.log('Selected hex:', hex.q, hex.r); // hex.q = axial q coordinate // hex.r = axial r coordinate // hex.terrain = optional terrain type // hex.data = custom attached metadata }); // Configure terrain and metadata grid.setHexTerrain(0, 0, 'GRASSLAND'); grid.setHexData(0, 0, { owner: 'blue', label: 'Spawn' }); // Update grid dynamically grid.setSize(40); grid.setDimensions(20, 15); grid.setRotation(-Math.PI / 6); // Pan and zoom are built-in // - Drag to pan // - Scroll to zoom // - Click to select a hex Full Source Code hex-grid.js Main VdHexGrid module: canvas rendering, pan/zoom, hex selection, terrain hooks, and events.. // VdHexGrid - Experimental interactive hex-grid toolkit for Vanduo framework // Based on web-civ HexGrid implementation // Intended for visualization, prototypes, and experimental game mechanics import { hexToPixel, pixelToHex, getHexCorners, getAdjacentHexes, hexDistance, TerrainType, isPassable, getMovementCost, getTerrainYields, getTerrainColor } from './utils/hex-math.js'; // Constants const ZOOM_MIN = 0.3; const ZOOM_MAX = 3.0; const ZOOM_FACTOR = 0.1; const DRAG_THRESHOLD = 2; /** * VdHexGrid - A dynamic controllable hex grid component * * @example * const grid = new VdHexGrid({ * element: document.getElementById('container'), * canvas: document.getElementById('canvas'), * size: 30, * width: 15, * height: 10, * rotation: 0 // Optional rotation in radians * }); * * grid.on('select', (hex) => { * console.log('Selected:', hex.q, hex.r); * }); */ export class VdHexGrid { constructor({ element, canvas, size = 30, width = 10, height = 10, rotation = 0 }) { this.element = element; this.canvas = canvas; this.size = size; this.width = width; this.height = height; this.rotation = rotation; this.hexes = new Map(); this.selectedHex = null; this.listeners = {}; // Transform state for pan/zoom this.transform = { x: 0, y: 0, scale: 1 }; // Drag state this.dragging = false; this.lastPos = null; this.hasMoved = false; // Theme colors this.themeColors = this._getThemeColors(); // Set up canvas if not already done if (!this.canvas) { this.canvas = element.querySelector('canvas') || document.createElement('canvas'); if (!element.contains(this.canvas)) { element.appendChild(this.canvas); } } this.ctx = this.canvas.getContext('2d'); // Generate the grid this._generateGrid(); this._render(); this._setupEvents(); // Observe theme changes this._observeThemeChanges(); } /** * Get theme colors from CSS custom properties */ _getThemeColors() { const root = document.documentElement; const style = getComputedStyle(root); return { bgPrimary: style.getPropertyValue('--bg-primary').trim() || '#ffffff', bgSecondary: style.getPropertyValue('--bg-secondary').trim() || '#f5f5f5', borderColor: style.getPropertyValue('--border-color').trim() || '#e0e0e0', colorPrimary: style.getPropertyValue('--color-primary').trim() || '#3b82f6', textColor: style.getPropertyValue('--text-primary').trim() || '#1f2937', textMuted: style.getPropertyValue('--text-muted').trim() || '#6b7280' }; } /** * Observe theme changes and re-render when theme changes */ _observeThemeChanges() { const observer = new MutationObserver(() => { this.themeColors = this._getThemeColors(); this._render(); }); observer.observe(document.documentElement, { attributes: true, attributeFilter: ['data-theme'] }); } /** * Convert screen coordinates to world coordinates */ _screenToWorld(screenX, screenY) { return { x: (screenX - this.transform.x) / this.transform.scale, y: (screenY - this.transform.y) / this.transform.scale }; } /** * Generate hex grid data */ _generateGrid() { this.hexes.clear(); // Calculate offset to center the grid const gridWidth = this.width * this.size * 1.5; const gridHeight = this.height * this.size * Math.sqrt(3); for (let r = 0; r { this._drawHex(hex); }); // Redraw selected hex if any if (this.selectedHex) { this._drawHex(this.selectedHex, true); } this.ctx.restore(); } /** * Draw a single hex */ _drawHex(hex, isSelected = false) { const corners = getHexCorners(hex.x, hex.y, this.size); this.ctx.beginPath(); this.ctx.moveTo(corners[0].x, corners[0].y); for (let i = 1; i { this.dragging = true; this.hasMoved = false; this.lastPos = { x: e.clientX, y: e.clientY }; this.canvas.style.cursor = 'grabbing'; }); // Pan - pointer move this.canvas.addEventListener('pointermove', (e) => { if (!this.dragging) return; const cur = { x: e.clientX, y: e.clientY }; const dx = cur.x - this.lastPos.x; const dy = cur.y - this.lastPos.y; if (Math.abs(dx) > DRAG_THRESHOLD || Math.abs(dy) > DRAG_THRESHOLD) { this.hasMoved = true; } this.transform.x += dx; this.transform.y += dy; this.lastPos = cur; this._render(); }); // Pan - pointer up const stopDrag = () => { this.dragging = false; if (!this.hasMoved) { this.canvas.style.cursor = 'pointer'; } }; this.canvas.addEventListener('pointerup', stopDrag); this.canvas.addEventListener('pointerleave', stopDrag); // Click (tap without drag) this.canvas.addEventListener('click', (e) => { if (this.hasMoved) return; const worldPos = this._screenToWorld(e.clientX, e.clientY); const hexCoords = pixelToHex(worldPos.x, worldPos.y, this.size); const hex = this.hexes.get(`${hexCoords.q},${hexCoords.r}`); if (hex) { this.selectedHex = hex; this._render(); this._emit('select', hex); } }); // Zoom this.canvas.addEventListener('wheel', (e) => { e.preventDefault(); const zoomFactor = e.deltaY > 0 ? 1 - ZOOM_FACTOR : 1 + ZOOM_FACTOR; const newScale = Math.max(ZOOM_MIN, Math.min(this.transform.scale * zoomFactor, ZOOM_MAX)); // Zoom toward cursor const mouseX = e.clientX; const mouseY = e.clientY; const scaleDiff = newScale / this.transform.scale; this.transform.x = mouseX - (mouseX - this.transform.x) * scaleDiff; this.transform.y = mouseY - (mouseY - this.transform.y) * scaleDiff; this.transform.scale = newScale; this._render(); this._emit('zoom', { scale: this.transform.scale }); }, { passive: false }); // Cursor style this.canvas.addEventListener('mouseenter', () => { this.canvas.style.cursor = 'grab'; }); this.canvas.addEventListener('mouseleave', () => { this.canvas.style.cursor = 'default'; }); } /** * Set hex size */ setSize(size) { this.size = size; this._generateGrid(); this._render(); } /** * Set grid dimensions */ setDimensions(width, height) { this.width = width; this.height = height; this._generateGrid(); this._render(); } /** * Reset grid to defaults */ reset() { this.size = 30; this.width = 15; this.height = 10; this.rotation = 0; this.selectedHex = null; this.transform = { x: 0, y: 0, scale: 1 }; this._generateGrid(); this._render(); } /** * Fill hexes with random colors */ fillRandom() { const colors = ['#f0f0f0', '#d4e5d4', '#e5d4d4', '#d4d4e5', '#e5e5d4', '#d4e5e5', '#e8e8e8', '#d0d0d0']; this.hexes.forEach(hex => { hex.fill = colors[Math.floor(Math.random() * colors.length)]; }); this._render(); } /** * Get hex by coordinates */ getHe

chunks.heading: vd-hex

chunks.text: An experimental interactive hex-grid toolkit with selection, pan/zoom, terrain helpers, and optional rotation. Click a hex to inspect it and use the controls below to explore the API.
chunks.heading: vd-hex Component

chunks.text: Standalone modules: github.com/vanduo-oss/labs .
chunks.heading: vd-hex Component

chunks.text: Minimal markup for the canvas container plus JavaScript to create the same interactive grid as above—expand to copy or compare with your own project.
chunks.heading: Usage Example

chunks.text: Main VdHexGrid module: canvas rendering, pan/zoom, hex selection, terrain hooks, and events.
chunks.heading: Full Source Code

chunks.text: // VdHexGrid - Experimental interactive hex-grid toolkit for Vanduo framework // Based on web-civ HexGrid implementation // Intended for visualization, prototypes, and experimental game mechanics import { hexToPixel, pixelToHex, getHexCorners, getAdjacentHexes, hexDistance, TerrainType, isPassable, getMovementCost, getTerrainYields, getTerrainColor } from './utils/hex-math.js'; // Constants const ZOOM_MIN = 0.3; const ZOOM_MAX = 3.0; const ZOOM_FACTOR = 0.1; const DRAG_THRESHOLD = 2; /** * VdHexGrid - A dynamic controllable hex grid component * * @example * const grid = new VdHexGrid({ * element: document.getElementById('container'), * canvas: document.getElementById('canvas'), * size: 30, * width: 15, * height: 10, * rotation: 0 // Optional rotation in radians * }); * * grid.on('select', (hex) => { * console.log('Selected:', hex.q, hex.r); * }); */ export class VdHexGrid { constructor({ element, canvas, size = 30, width = 10, height = 10, rotation = 0 }) { this.element = element; this.canvas = canvas; this.size = size; this.width = width; this.height = height; this.rotation = rotation; this.hexes = new Map(); this.selectedHex = null; this.listeners = {}; // Transform state for pan/zoom this.transform = { x: 0, y: 0, scale: 1 }; // Drag state this.dragging = false; this.lastPos = null; this.hasMoved = false; // Theme colors this.themeColors = this._getThemeColors(); // Set up canvas if not already done if (!this.canvas) { this.canvas = element.querySelector('canvas') || document.createElement('canvas'); if (!element.contains(this.canvas)) { element.appendChild(this.canvas); } } this.ctx = this.canvas.getContext('2d'); // Generate the grid this._generateGrid(); this._render(); this._setupEvents(); // Observe theme changes this._observeThemeChanges(); } /** * Get theme colors from CSS custom properties */ _getThemeColors() { const root = document.documentElement; const style = getComputedStyle(root); return { bgPrimary: style.getPropertyValue('--bg-primary').trim() || '#ffffff', bgSecondary: style.getPropertyValue('--bg-secondary').trim() || '#f5f5f5', borderColor: style.getPropertyValue('--border-color').trim() || '#e0e0e0', colorPrimary: style.getPropertyValue('--color-primary').trim() || '#3b82f6', textColor: style.getPropertyValue('--text-primary').trim() || '#1f2937', textMuted: style.getPropertyValue('--text-muted').trim() || '#6b7280' }; } /** * Observe theme changes and re-render when theme changes */ _observeThemeChanges() { const observer = new MutationObserver(() => { this.themeColors = this._getThemeColors(); this._render(); }); observer.observe(document.documentElement, { attributes: true, attributeFilter: ['data-theme'] }); } /** * Convert screen coordinates to world coordinates */ _screenToWorld(screenX, screenY) { return { x: (screenX - this.transform.x) / this.transform.scale, y: (screenY - this.transform.y) / this.transform.scale }; } /** * Generate hex grid data */ _generateGrid() { this.hexes.clear(); // Calculate offset to center the grid const gridWidth = this.width * this.size * 1.5; const gridHeight = this.height * this.size * Math.sqrt(3); for (let r = 0; r { this._drawHex(hex); }); // Redraw selected hex if any if (this.selectedHex) { this._drawHex(this.selectedHex, true); } this.ctx.restore(); } /** * Draw a single hex */ _drawHex(hex, isSelected = false) { const corners = getHexCorners(hex.x, hex.y, this.size); this.ctx.beginPath(); this.ctx.moveTo(corners[0].x, corners[0].y); for (let i = 1; i { this.dragging = true; this.hasMoved = false; this.lastPos = { x: e.clientX, y: e.clientY }; this.canvas.style.cursor = 'grabbing'; }); // Pan - pointer move this.canvas.addEventListener('pointermove', (e) => { if (!this.dragging) return; const cur = { x: e.clientX, y: e.clientY }; const dx = cur.x - this.lastPos.x; const dy = cur.y - this.lastPos.y; if (Math.abs(dx) > DRAG_THRESHOLD || Math.abs(dy) > DRAG_THRESHOLD) { this.hasMoved = true; } this.transform.x += dx; this.transform.y += dy; this.lastPos = cur; this._render(); }); // Pan - pointer up const stopDrag = () => { this.dragging = false; if (!this.hasMoved) { this.canvas.style.cursor = 'pointer'; } }; this.canvas.addEventListener('pointerup', stopDrag); this.canvas.addEventListener('pointerleave', stopDrag); // Click (tap without drag) this.canvas.addEventListener('click', (e) => { if (this.hasMoved) return; const worldPos = this._screenToWorld(e.clientX, e.clientY); const hexCoords = pixelToHex(worldPos.x, worldPos.y, this.size); const hex = this.hexes.get(`${hexCoords.q},${hexCoords.r}`); if (hex) { this.selectedHex = hex; this._render(); this._emit('select', hex); } }); // Zoom this.canvas.addEventListener('wheel', (e) => { e.preventDefault(); const zoomFactor = e.deltaY > 0 ? 1 - ZOOM_FACTOR : 1 + ZOOM_FACTOR; const newScale = Math.max(ZOOM_MIN, Math.min(this.transform.scale * zoomFactor, ZOOM_MAX)); // Zoom toward cursor const mouseX = e.clientX; const mouseY = e.clientY; const scaleDiff = newScale / this.transform.scale; this.transform.x = mouseX - (mouseX - this.transform.x) * scaleDiff; this.transform.y = mouseY - (mouseY - this.transform.y) * scaleDiff; this.transform.scale = newScale; this._render(); this._emit('zoom', { scale: this.transform.scale }); }, { passive: false }); // Cursor style this.canvas.addEventListener('mouseenter', () => { this.canvas.style.cursor = 'grab'; }); this.canvas.addEventListener('mouseleave', () => { this.canvas.style.cursor = 'default'; }); } /** * Set hex size */ setSize(size) { this.size = size; this._generateGrid(); this._render(); } /** * Set grid dimensions */ setDimensions(width, height) { this.width = width; this.height = height; this._generateGrid(); this._render(); } /** * Reset grid to defaults */ reset() { this.size = 30; this.width = 15; this.height = 10; this.rotation = 0; this.selectedHex = null; this.transform = { x: 0, y: 0, scale: 1 }; this._generateGrid(); this._render(); } /** * Fill hexes with random colors */ fillRandom() { const colors = ['#f0f0f0', '#d4e5d4', '#e5d4d4', '#d4d4e5', '#e5e5d4', '#d4e5e5', '#e8e8e8', '#d0d0d0']; this.hexes.forEach(hex => { hex.fill = colors[Math.floor(Math.random() * colors.length)]; }); this._render(); } /** * Get hex by coordinates */ getHex(q, r) { return this.hexes.get(`${q},${r}`); } /** * Get all hexes */ getAllHexes() { return Array.from(this.hexes.values()); } /** * Set hex fill color */ setHexFill(q, r, color) { const hex = this.hexes.get(`${q},${r}`); if (hex) { hex.fill = color; this._render(); } } /** * Reset view to default position */ resetView() { this.transform = { x: 0, y: 0, scale: 1 }; this._render(); this._emit('pan', { x: 0, y: 0 }); this._emit('zoom', { scale: 1 }); } /** * Zoom in */ zoomIn() { const newScale = Math.min(this.transform.scale * (1 + ZOOM_FACTOR), ZOOM_MAX); this.transform.scale = newScale; this._render(); this._emit('zoom', { scale: this.transform.scale }); } /** * Zoom out */ zoomOut() { const newScale = Math.max(this.transform.scale * (1 - ZOOM_FACTOR), ZOOM_MIN); this.transform.scale = newScale; this._render(); this._emit('zoom', { scale: this.transform.scale }); } /** * Get current transform state */ getTransform() { return { ...this.transform }; } /** * Subscribe to events */ on(event, callback) { if (!this.listeners[event]) { this.listeners[event] = []; } this.listeners[event].push(callback); } /** * Emit events */ _emit(event, data) { if (this.listeners[event]) { this.listeners[event].forEach(callback => callback(data)); } } } hex-math.js Axial coordinate helpers: hexToPixel , pixelToHex , corner geometry, and neighbor lists.

chunks.text: VdHexGrid uses axial coordinates (q, r) to identify hexagons. This is a flat-top hexagon orientation where:
chunks.heading: Hex Coordinate System

chunks.text: r ↑ │ ╱ ╲ │ ╱ ╲ │ ╱ ╲ │╱ ╲ ┼─────────→ q │╲ ╱ │ ╲ ╱ │ ╲ ╱ │ ╲ ╱ Adjacent Hexes (6 directions): East: (q+1, r) Northeast: (q+1, r-1) Northwest: (q, r-1) West: (q-1, r) Southwest: (q-1, r+1) Southeast: (q, r+1) Why axial coordinates? They provide a simple, efficient way to represent hex grids using just two integers. Distance calculations, neighbor finding, and pathfinding all work naturally with this system.
chunks.heading: Hex Coordinate System

chunks.text: Distance formula: distance = (|q1-q2| + |q1+r1-q2-r2| + |r1-r2|) / 2
chunks.heading: Hex Coordinate System

chunks.text: q - Column coordinate (horizontal axis)
chunks.heading: Hex Coordinate System

chunks.text: r - Row coordinate (diagonal axis)
chunks.heading: Hex Coordinate System

chunks.text: East: (q+1, r)
chunks.heading: Hex Coordinate System

chunks.text: Northeast: (q+1, r-1)
chunks.heading: Hex Coordinate System

chunks.text: Northwest: (q, r-1)
chunks.heading: Hex Coordinate System

chunks.text: West: (q-1, r)
chunks.heading: Hex Coordinate System

chunks.text: Southwest: (q-1, r+1)
chunks.heading: Hex Coordinate System

chunks.text: Southeast: (q, r+1)
chunks.heading: Hex Coordinate System

chunks.text: VdHexGrid includes terrain primitives with 8 terrain types, each with colors, yields, and movement-cost metadata suitable for experimentation and prototyping.
chunks.heading: Terrain System

chunks.text: Create a Civilization-style strategy game with terrain, cities, and units.
chunks.heading: Use Cases

chunks.text: // Create game map const grid = new VdHexGrid({ element: document.getElementById('game-container'), canvas: document.getElementById('game-canvas'), size: 40, width: 20, height: 15 }); // Generate terrain grid.generateRandomTerrain(); // Add game data to hexes grid.setHexData(5, 5, { city: 'Capital', owner: 'red' }); grid.setHexData(10, 8, { unit: 'Warrior', owner: 'blue' }); // Handle hex selection grid.on('select', (hex) => { if (hex.data.city) { showCityPanel(hex.data.city); } else if (hex.data.unit) { showUnitPanel(hex.data.unit); } }); 2. Board Game Visualization Visualize hex-based board games like Settlers of Catan or Terra Mystica.
chunks.heading: Use Cases

chunks.text: // Create board game grid const grid = new VdHexGrid({ element: document.getElementById('board'), canvas: document.getElementById('board-canvas'), size: 50, width: 7, height: 7 }); // Set terrain types for each hex grid.setHexTerrain(0, 0, 'DESERT'); grid.setHexTerrain(1, 0, 'GRASSLAND'); grid.setHexTerrain(2, 0, 'PLAINS'); // ... etc // Add resource tokens grid.setHexData(1, 0, { resource: 'wood', token: 8 }); grid.setHexData(2, 0, { resource: 'brick', token: 5 }); 3. Data Visualization Create hex-based heatmaps or geographic data visualizations.
chunks.heading: Use Cases

chunks.text: VdHexGrid is optimized for performance, but large grids can impact rendering speed.
chunks.heading: Performance

chunks.text: Limit grid size: Keep grids under 10,000 hexes for smooth performance
chunks.heading: Performance

chunks.text: Use terrain colors: Terrain-based rendering is faster than custom fills
chunks.heading: Performance

chunks.text: Minimize re-renders: Batch updates when possible
chunks.heading: Performance

chunks.text: Use custom render callbacks sparingly: They're called for every hex
chunks.heading: Performance

chunks.text: Consider WebGL: For very large grids (>50,000 hexes), consider WebGL rendering
chunks.heading: Performance

chunks.text: A self-contained hex grid toolkit built for the Vanduo framework — no external dependencies, ready for games and data visualisation alike.
chunks.heading: What VdHexGrid Offers

chunks.text: Pure vanilla JS — no npm runtime packages required.
chunks.heading: What VdHexGrid Offers

chunks.text: Every hex is drawn on a <canvas> for smooth, pixel-perfect output.
chunks.heading: What VdHexGrid Offers

chunks.text: Mouse wheel zoom, pointer drag pan, and pinch-to-zoom on touch devices.
chunks.heading: What VdHexGrid Offers

chunks.text: Eight terrain types with colours, movement costs, and resource yields built in.
chunks.heading: What VdHexGrid Offers

chunks.text: Reads Vanduo CSS custom properties and re-renders automatically when the theme changes.
chunks.heading: What VdHexGrid Offers

chunks.text: BFS path search and movement-range helpers respect terrain passability costs.
chunks.heading: What VdHexGrid Offers

chunks.text: Touch events and Pointer API provide a consistent experience across all devices.
chunks.heading: What VdHexGrid Offers

chunks.text: ~15 KB minified & gzipped — easy to drop into any project without bloat.
chunks.heading: What VdHexGrid Offers

documents.title: Kilo OSS
documents.bodyText: Our open-source journey and Kilo OSS sponsorship.. Vanduo Framework was selected for the Kilo Open Source Sponsorship Program at the Seed tier .. The sponsorship includes five enterprise seats for AI-assisted code review, helping us keep pull requests consistent and maintain a fast feedback loop.. Open-source projects thrive when maintainers can spend more time shipping, less time stuck in maintenance overhead. Sponsorship support allows us to keep improving docs, harden core components, and respond to community feedback faster.. Vanduo remains fully open source, MIT licensed, and community-driven. If you are building with Vanduo, contributing examples, or reporting issues, you are part of this story too.

documents.title: Icons
documents.bodyText: Vanduo includes Phosphor Icons — a flexible icon family with 1,500+ icons in 6 weights. Icons are bundled locally with no external dependencies.. 6 distinct weights for different visual styles:. Regular .ph. Fill .ph-fill. Bold .ph-bold. Light .ph-light. Thin .ph-thin. Duotone .ph-duotone. Note: Only regular and fill weights are loaded by default. Other weights require importing their CSS.. Include the icons stylesheet and use <i> elements with the appropriate classes:. <!-- Include default weights (regular + fill) --> < link rel = "stylesheet" href = "css/icons/icons.css" > <!-- Use icons --> < i class = "ph ph-heart" ></ i > < i class = "ph-fill ph-star" ></ i > < i class = "ph ph-house" ></ i > Import Options View Code HTML Copy <!-- Default (regular + fill) - Recommended --> < link rel = "stylesheet" href = "css/icons/icons.css" > <!-- All 6 weights (~3MB) --> < link rel = "stylesheet" href = "css/icons/icons-all.css" > <!-- Individual weights --> < link rel = "stylesheet" href = "icons/phosphor/bold/style.css" > < link rel = "stylesheet" href = "icons/phosphor/light/style.css" > Example Icons Browse all 1,500+ icons at phosphoricons.com. Icons are font-based — style them with CSS:. <!-- Size with font-size --> < i class = "ph ph-heart" style = "font-size: 48px;" ></ i > <!-- Color with color --> < i class = "ph-fill ph-star" style = "color: gold;" ></ i > <!-- Use CSS variables --> < i class = "ph ph-check" style = "color: var(--color-success);" ></ i > Icons in Buttons Download Save Delete Settings Like Share Icons by Phosphor Icons — MIT Licensed

documents.title: Color Palette
documents.bodyText: Vanduo uses the Open Color palette (MIT License) — a battle-tested, accessible color system with 10-step scales for each hue. Perfect for the "no-build" philosophy.. The "Water" brand color for Vanduo. Complementary to Cyan for secondary actions. Used for backgrounds, borders, text, and UI structure. Error states, destructive actions. Success states, confirmations. Warnings, cautions, attention. Information, help, neutral feedback. Apply background and text colors directly via CSS classes — no custom styles needed. v1.2.7. Every Open Color hue (0–9 shades) is available as a utility class. The pattern maps directly to the CSS custom property names.. Use vd-bg-{color}-{shade} to set an element's background color.. < div class = "vd-bg-teal-3" >...</ div > Text Color Use vd-text-{color}-{shade} to set an element's text color.. < span class = "vd-text-blue-7" >...</ span > Combining Background & Text Stack both classes on a single element for full control.. < div class = "vd-bg-indigo-8 vd-text-yellow-2" >...</ div > Available Hues All 13 Open Color hues are supported, each with shades 0 (lightest) through 9 (darkest):. /* Direct scale access */ .my-element { background : var(--primary-0) ; /* Lightest */ color : var(--primary-9) ; /* Darkest */ } /* Semantic aliases (recommended) */ .my-button { background : var(--color-primary) ; border-color : var(--color-primary-dark) ; } /* Hover states using scale */ .my-button:hover { background : var(--primary-6) ; } .my-button:active { background : var(--primary-7) ; } Theming Guide To change the primary color to another Open Color hue (e.g., Violet):. :root { /* Swap Cyan with Violet */ --primary-0 : #f3f0ff ; --primary-1 : #e5dbff ; --primary-2 : #d0bfff ; --primary-3 : #b197fc ; --primary-4 : #9775fa ; --primary-5 : #845ef7 ; --primary-6 : #7950f2 ; --primary-7 : #7048e8 ; --primary-8 : #6741d9 ; --primary-9 : #5f3dc4 ; } All components using --primary-* or --color-primary will automatically update.. Color palette based on Open Color by Heeyeun Jeong — MIT Licensed

documents.title: Typography
documents.bodyText: Vanduo includes 10 carefully selected font families , all self-hosted for privacy and performance. No external font dependencies or Google Fonts requests.. Use the data-font attribute on the HTML element:. <!-- Set font on HTML element --> < html data-font = "ubuntu" > <!-- Or use the Theme Customizer (paint roller icon) to preview and select fonts --> < button data-theme-customizer-trigger > < i class = "ph ph-paint-roller" ></ i > </ button > Font preference is persisted to localStorage and restored on page load.. Need more fonts? You have two options:. Download fonts from Google Fonts and add them to your fonts/ folder. Follow the same pattern as existing fonts:. 1. Create folder: fonts/my-font/ 2. Add woff2 files 3. Add @font-face rules to css/core/fonts.css 4. Add [data-font="my-font"] selector to css/core/typography.css 5. Register in js/components/font-switcher.js Option 2: Google Fonts CDN For quick prototyping, use Google Fonts CDN link in your HTML head:. < link rel = "preconnect" href = "https://fonts.googleapis.com" > < link href = "https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Roboto:wght@400;500;700&display=swap" rel = "stylesheet" > < style > [data-font="roboto"] { --font-family-sans : 'Roboto', sans-serif ; } </ style > Headings Heading 1 Heading 2 Heading 3 Heading 4 Heading 5 Heading 6 Text Utilities Primary text color. Secondary text color. Success text color. Warning text color. Error text color. Info text color. Muted text color. Small text. Large text. Bold text. Italic text. Inter — Modern, clean sans-serif by Rasmus Andersson. Source Sans 3 — Adobe's versatile sans-serif. Fira Sans — Mozilla's readable sans-serif. IBM Plex Sans — Professional, complete font family. JetBrains Mono — Monospace font optimized for code. Ubuntu — Humanist sans-serif, friendly and approachable. Open Sans — Neutral sans-serif, highly readable. Rubik — Geometric sans-serif, modern and distinctive. Titillium Web — Technical, elegant sans-serif

documents.title: Grid System
documents.bodyText: A comprehensive 12-column flexbox grid with Fibonacci/Golden Ratio proportional layouts, responsive containers, order utilities, and an interactive toggle between standard and Fibonacci modes.. The grid now supports all 6 breakpoints: base, sm (576px), md (768px), lg (992px), xl (1200px), and 2xl (1400px).. <!-- Responsive Grid with 6 Breakpoints --> < div class = "vd-row" > < div class = "vd-col-12 vd-col-sm-6 vd-col-md-4 vd-col-lg-3 vd-col-xl-2 vd-col-2xl-1" > Column </ div > </ div > Responsive Containers Responsive containers are fluid below their breakpoint and fixed-width above. They scale up at each subsequent breakpoint.. <!-- Responsive Containers --> < div class = "vd-container-sm" >Fixed from 576px</ div > < div class = "vd-container-md" >Fixed from 768px</ div > < div class = "vd-container-lg" >Fixed from 992px</ div > < div class = "vd-container-xl" >Fixed from 1200px</ div > < div class = "vd-container-2xl" >Fixed from 1400px</ div > Order Utilities Reorder columns visually without changing HTML source order. Responsive variants available at all breakpoints.. Ratio 1:2:3:5 (total 11 parts). Use .vd-row-fib-4 with .vd-col-fib-1 , .vd-col-fib-2 , .vd-col-fib-3 , .vd-col-fib-5 .. Ratio 3:5:8 (total 16 parts). Use .vd-row-fib-3-alt with .vd-col-fib-3 , .vd-col-fib-5 , .vd-col-fib-8 .. Gap utilities using Fibonacci values. Works with CSS Grid and flex containers using the gap property.. Toggle between standard 12-column flexbox and Fibonacci CSS Grid proportions. Uses data-layout-mode attribute with a data-grid-toggle button. The Fibonacci mode auto-detects column count and applies golden ratio (2 cols), 2:3:5 (3 cols), or 1:2:3:5 (4 cols) proportions.. 3-column row:. 2-column row (becomes golden ratio):. 4-column row (becomes 1:2:3:5):. Complete offset utilities from 0-11 at all breakpoints. Use .vd-offset-0 to reset an offset at a specific breakpoint.

documents.title: Golden Ratio
documents.bodyText: This framework uses the Fibonacci sequence and Golden Ratio (phi = 1.618) as its proportional foundation. Spacing, typography, component dimensions, and grid layouts all derive from these mathematical relationships.. <!-- Golden Split Layout (38.2% / 61.8%) --> < div class = "vd-row-golden" > < div class = "vd-col-golden-minor" > < div class = "vd-bg-secondary vd-p-6 vd-rounded-md vd-text-center" > < strong >Minor</ strong >< br >38.2% (1/phi) </ div > </ div > < div class = "vd-col-golden-major" > < div class = "vd-bg-primary vd-text-white vd-p-6 vd-rounded-md vd-text-center" > < strong >Major</ strong >< br >61.8% (1 - 1/phi) </ div > </ div > </ div > Fibonacci 3-Column (2:3:5) 2 3 5 View Code HTML Copy <!-- Fibonacci 3-Column Layout (2:3:5 ratio) --> < div class = "vd-row-fib-3" > < div class = "vd-col-fib-2" >2</ div > < div class = "vd-col-fib-3" >3</ div > < div class = "vd-col-fib-5" >5</ div > </ div > CSS Grid Golden Layout 1fr 1.618fr View Code HTML Copy <!-- CSS Grid Golden Layout (1:1.618 ratio) --> < div class = "vd-grid-golden" > < div >1fr</ div > < div >1.618fr</ div > </ div > Fibonacci Spacing Scale 1px 2px 3px 5px 8px 13px 21px 34px 55px 89px 144px View Code CSS Copy /* Fibonacci Spacing Scale CSS Variables */ :root { --space-1 : 1 px ; --space-2 : 2 px ; --space-3 : 3 px ; --space-5 : 5 px ; --space-8 : 8 px ; --space-13 : 13 px ; --space-21 : 21 px ; --space-34 : 34 px ; --space-55 : 55 px ; --space-89 : 89 px ; --space-144 : 144 px ; } Golden Ratio Type Scale xs — base / phi. sm — base / sqrt(phi). base — 16px. lg — base x sqrt(phi). xl — base x phi. 2xl — base x phi^2

documents.title: Shadows & Glow
documents.bodyText: Vanduo provides a comprehensive shadow system based on Fibonacci proportions, plus glow effects for interactive elements. Shadows create depth and visual hierarchy, while glow effects add emphasis on hover.. Apply shadows with utility classes:. Add glowing effects to any element:. Control the glow intensity with size variants:. <!-- Basic glow on any element --> < div class = "vd-hover-glow" >Hover for primary glow</ div > <!-- Glow with specific color --> < button class = "vd-btn vd-btn-success vd-hover-glow-success" >Success Glow</ button > < button class = "vd-btn vd-btn-warning vd-hover-glow-warning" >Warning Glow</ button > < button class = "vd-btn vd-btn-error vd-hover-glow-error" >Error Glow</ button > <!-- Glow intensity variants --> < div class = "vd-hover-glow-sm" >Subtle glow</ div > < div class = "vd-hover-glow-md" >Medium glow</ div > < div class = "vd-hover-glow-lg" >Strong glow</ div > Colored Shadows Semantic color shadows for emphasis:. Customize glow colors via CSS variables:. :root { /* Glow colors */ --glow-primary-color : rgba(34, 184, 207, 0.25) ; --glow-success-color : rgba(64, 192, 87, 0.25) ; --glow-warning-color : rgba(250, 176, 5, 0.25) ; --glow-error-color : rgba(250, 82, 82, 0.25) ; /* Card glow (uses primary by default) */ --card-glow-color : rgba(34, 184, 207, 0.25) ; --card-glow-border-color : rgba(34, 184, 207, 0.4) ; } Note: Glow intensity is automatically increased in dark mode for better visibility.

documents.title: Theme Customizer
documents.bodyText: The Theme Customizer is a powerful component that allows users to personalize the framework's appearance in real-time. It provides controls for primary color, neutral color, border radius, font family, and color mode (light/dark/system).. Try all the theme options below. Changes apply immediately:. Add a trigger button anywhere in your page to open the customizer panel:. <!-- Trigger button --> < button class = "vd-theme-customizer-trigger" data-theme-customizer-trigger aria-label = "Open theme customizer" > < i class = "ph ph-paint-roller" ></ i > </ button > < !-- Include the JS component --> < script src = "js/components/theme-customizer.js" ></ script > Try it: Click the icon in the navbar to open the Theme Customizer.. All preferences are persisted to localStorage and restored on page load.. Neutral colors affect backgrounds, borders, and text throughout the framework.. // Access the component const customizer = Vanduo.components.themeCustomizer; // Open/close programmatically customizer. open (); customizer. close (); customizer. toggle (); // Get current state const state = customizer. getState (); // Returns: { primary, neutral, radius, font, theme } // Set values programmatically customizer. applyPrimary ( 'violet' ); customizer. applyNeutral ( 'slate' ); customizer. applyRadius ( '0.375' ); customizer. applyFont ( 'inter' ); customizer. applyTheme ( 'dark' ); // Or update multiple preferences at once customizer. setPreferences ({ primary : 'violet' , neutral : 'slate' , radius : '0.375' , font : 'inter' , theme : 'dark' }); // Reset to defaults customizer. reset (); API Reference Class Name / Attribute Description Type .vd-theme-customizer-trigger Optional base styling component for the trigger button. Component data-theme-customizer-trigger Attribute required on the trigger button to bind the click event that opens the panel. Attribute data-primary Applied to <html> . Remaps --primary-* CSS variables to the selected color. State Attribute data-neutral Applied to <html> . Remaps --gray-* CSS variables to the selected neutral scale. State Attribute data-radius Applied to <html> . Sets --radius-scale for component border-radius. State Attribute data-font Applied to <html> . Sets --font-family-base to the selected font stack. State Attribute data-theme Applied to <html> . Forces light or dark mode. Removed when using system preference. State Attribute Coordination with ThemeSwitcher Auto-Coordination When both ThemeCustomizer and ThemeSwitcher are present on the same page, they automatically stay in sync:. button class = "vd-btn vd-btn-icon" data-toggle = "theme" > i class = "ph ph-moon" > i > button > button class = "vd-theme-customizer-trigger" data-theme-customizer-trigger > i class = "ph ph-paint-roller" > i > button > CSS Customization Override the customizer panel styles:. /* Panel dimensions */ .theme-customizer-panel { --tc-panel-width : 320px ; --tc-panel-max-height : 90vh ; } /* Trigger button */ .theme-customizer-trigger { --tc-trigger-size : 40px ; --tc-trigger-bg : var(--bg-secondary) ; } /* Color swatches */ .tc-color-swatch { --tc-swatch-size : 28px ; } Events Listen for customizer events:. Primary Color: 18 color options from the Open Color palette. Neutral Color: 5 gray scale variants (Gray, Slate, Zinc, Neutral, Stone). Border Radius: 5 presets (0, 0.125rem, 0.25rem, 0.375rem, 0.5rem). Font Family: 10 bundled fonts including JetBrains Mono (default) plus Google Fonts collection. Color Mode: System (default), Dark, or Light. Changing theme via ThemeSwitcher button triggers primary color swap if using defaults. Changing theme via ThemeCustomizer panel updates ThemeSwitcher state. Preferences are shared via localStorage

documents.title: Theme Switcher
documents.bodyText: The Theme Switcher is a lightweight component for toggling between light, dark, and system color modes. Unlike the full Theme Customizer, it focuses solely on the color mode toggle without additional customization options. Perfect for when you need a simple dark mode button.. Click any button below to change the theme:. Current theme: system. Add data-toggle="theme" to any button to enable theme cycling:. button class = "vd-btn vd-btn-icon" data-toggle = "theme" aria-label = "Toggle theme" > i class = "ph ph-moon" > i > button > button class = "vd-btn vd-btn-secondary" data-toggle = "theme" > Theme: span class = "theme-current-label" >System span > button > script src = "js/components/theme-switcher.js" > script > Try it: Click the theme button in the navbar to cycle through System → Light → Dark → System.. Use a select element for explicit theme selection:. select class = "vd-input" data-toggle = "theme" > option value = "system" >System option > option value = "light" >Light option > option value = "dark" >Dark option > select > ThemeSwitcher vs ThemeCustomizer Feature ThemeSwitcher ThemeCustomizer Light/Dark/System mode Per-theme primary colors (follows system) Color palette selection (18 colors) Neutral scale (5 options) Border radius Font family File size ~3KB ~15KB Use case Simple toggle Full customization Per-Theme Primary Colors When using ThemeCustomizer with ThemeSwitcher, you can define different primary colors for light and dark modes:. // Override defaults before init Object. assign (window.ThemeCustomizer.DEFAULTS, { // Primary color in light mode PRIMARY_LIGHT: 'black' , // Primary color in dark mode PRIMARY_DARK: 'amber' }); // Initialize Vanduo Vanduo. init (); When the user switches themes, the primary color automatically changes if they're using the default. If they've manually selected a color, it stays unchanged.. // Access the component const switcher = Vanduo.components.themeSwitcher; // Get current preference const pref = switcher. getPreference (); // Returns: 'system', 'light', or 'dark' // Set preference programmatically switcher. setPreference ( 'dark' ); // Apply without changing preference switcher. applyTheme (); localStorage Key Key Default Values vanduo-theme-preference system system, light, dark The preference is automatically saved and restored on page load.. You only need light/dark toggle. File size is critical. Quick integration needed. Navbar space is limited. Users need color customization. You want per-theme primary colors. Font/radius customization needed. Building a theme-aware app. Quick toggle in navbar. Full panel in settings. Auto-coordination provided. Best of both worlds. Coordination with ThemeCustomizer: When both components are present, they automatically stay in sync. Changing the theme via ThemeSwitcher will also swap the primary color if you're using the default per-theme colors (PRIMARY_LIGHT / PRIMARY_DARK).

chunks.text: The Theme Switcher is a lightweight component for toggling between light, dark, and system color modes. Unlike the full Theme Customizer, it focuses solely on the color mode toggle without additional customization options. Perfect for when you need a simple dark mode button.

documents.title: Cards
documents.bodyText: Cards group content with consistent padding, borders, and elevation. Combine variants with .vd-card-glow , .vd-card-glass , or the Water Morph effect for richer demos.. Elevated is the default: medium shadow (stronger in dark mode). Outlined drops the shadow for a crisp border. Filled uses a subtle surface tint and no border.. Default elevated surface — compare the shadow to outlined and filled.. Border only, no drop shadow — ideal on busy backgrounds.. Secondary background fill, no border — reads as a soft panel.. Add .vd-card-glow for a soft primary-tinted halo and border shift on hover (in addition to the elevated hover lift when applicable).. Hover this card. Glow uses --card-glow-color / --card-glow-border-color .. .vd-card-glass applies the glass token stack from the Glass Effects system — best seen over imagery or gradients. Respects prefers-reduced-transparency via the glass utilities.. Backdrop blur and translucent fill let the stage show through.. Pair a card shell with the Water Morph effect to swap summary ↔ detail. Uses .vd-morph-lg for a slower wave.. .vd-card-compact tightens header, body, and footer padding.. .vd-card-spacious uses larger Fibonacci padding for airy layouts.
