documents.title: Image Box
documents.bodyText: A smooth "lightbox" implementation for enlarging images. Supports dual-image mode with separate thumbnails and full-size images. When users click on an image, Image Box centers it and smoothly scales it up. Dismiss by clicking, pressing ESC, or scrolling away.. Thumbnail → Full-size on click. This gallery demonstrates the dual-image feature. Small thumbnails are displayed, but clicking opens the full-resolution version. Includes landscape and portrait orientations.. Add the data-image-box attribute to any image you want to make enlargeable:. The component auto-initializes on page load. No JavaScript setup required.. Create responsive image galleries using the .image-box-gallery class. The grid automatically adjusts columns based on available space.. The gallery uses CSS Grid with auto-fit columns (minimum 120px). Add data-orientation="portrait" for 3:4 aspect ratio images.. // Access the component const imageBox = Vanduo.components.imageBox; // Open an image programmatically imageBox.open(document.querySelector('[data-image-box]')); // Close the image box imageBox.close(); // Re-initialize after adding new images to DOM Vanduo.reinit('imageBox'); // Destroy and clean up imageBox.destroy(); CSS Customization Override CSS variables to customize the appearance:. Dual Image Support: Use data-image-box-full-src for separate thumbnail and full-size images. Smooth Transitions: CSS-powered scale animation with cubic-bezier easing. Zoom Cursor: Magnifying glass icon on hover indicates enlargement. Multiple Close Methods: Click image, press ESC, or scroll away. Scroll Detection: Auto-closes when scrolling more than 50px. Backdrop Blur: Beautiful frosted glass effect behind enlarged image. Captions: Optional captions with data-image-box-caption. Accessible: Full keyboard navigation and ARIA attributes

chunks.text: A smooth "lightbox" implementation for enlarging images. Supports dual-image mode with separate thumbnails and full-size images. When users click on an image, Image Box centers it and smoothly scales it up. Dismiss by clicking, pressing ESC, or scrolling away.

documents.title: Music Player
documents.bodyText: A fully accessible HTML5 audio player component with transport controls, volume, and optional shuffle, seek bar, and playlist features. Zero external dependencies, powered by the native Audio API, and it automatically adapts to the active Vanduo theme.. To keep the docs lightweight, all live demos below use one bundled sample track: Pale Blue Dot . In real usage, pass as many tracks as you need.. Default config — play/pause, prev/next, volume, track name. < div id = "my-player" class = "vd-music-player" ></ div > const tracks = [ { name: 'Pale Blue Dot' , url: './music/Stellardrone/Invent the Universe/06 - Pale Blue Dot.mp3' , }, ]; VanduoMusicPlayer.initPlayer( document.getElementById( 'my-player' ), { tracks } ); With Progress Bar Enable showProgress: true for a seek bar + elapsed/duration times. VanduoMusicPlayer.initPlayer(el, { tracks, showProgress: true , }); With Shuffle Start shuffled with shuffle: true , or tap the shuffle button to toggle. The docs demo uses one bundled track, but the option works the same for longer lists.. VanduoMusicPlayer.initPlayer(el, { tracks, shuffle: true , }); With Playlist Enable showPlaylist: true to reveal a collapsible track list. The bundled docs sample shows the UI using one tracked song.. VanduoMusicPlayer.initPlayer(el, { tracks, showPlaylist: true , }); Compact Minimal floating bar, no track name shown. < div class = "vd-music-player vd-music-player-compact" ></ div > Small View Code HTML Copy < div class = "vd-music-player vd-music-player-sm" ></ div > Large View Code HTML Copy < div class = "vd-music-player vd-music-player-lg" ></ div > Inline Layout — All Features Full-width bar with progress bar, shuffle and playlist. < div id = "my-player" class = "vd-music-player vd-music-player-inline" ></ div > VanduoMusicPlayer.initPlayer( document.getElementById( 'my-player' ), { tracks, showProgress: true , showPlaylist: true , shuffle: false , } ); Programmatic API View Code JS Copy const el = document.getElementById( 'my-player' ); // Transport VanduoMusicPlayer.play(el); VanduoMusicPlayer.pause(el); VanduoMusicPlayer.toggle(el); VanduoMusicPlayer.next(el); VanduoMusicPlayer.previous(el); // Go to a specific track (0-based index) VanduoMusicPlayer.setTrack(el, 0 ); // Volume (0 to 1) VanduoMusicPlayer.setVolume(el, 0.75 ); // Toggle shuffle programmatically VanduoMusicPlayer.shuffle(el); // Get current state const state = VanduoMusicPlayer.getState(el); // { isPlaying, currentIndex, currentTrack, volume, shuffle, tracks } // Tear down VanduoMusicPlayer.destroy(el); VanduoMusicPlayer.destroyAll(); // Re-init after dynamic markup changes Vanduo.reinit( 'musicPlayer' ); Custom Events View Code JS Copy el.addEventListener( 'musicplayer:play' , () => { console.log( 'Playback started' ); }); el.addEventListener( 'musicplayer:pause' , () => { console.log( 'Playback paused' ); }); el.addEventListener( 'musicplayer:trackchange' , ( event ) => { const { index, name, url } = event.detail; console.log( 'Track changed to:' , index, name, url); }); el.addEventListener( 'musicplayer:volumechange' , ( event ) => { console.log( 'Volume:' , event.detail.volume); }); el.addEventListener( 'musicplayer:ended' , () => { // Fires only when autoAdvance is false on the last track. console.log( 'Playback ended' ); }); CSS Variables View Code CSS Copy :root { /* Accent */ --music-player-accent : var(--color-primary) ; /* Surfaces */ --music-player-bg : var(--bg-primary) ; --music-player-bg-secondary : var(--bg-secondary) ; --music-player-border : var(--border-color) ; /* Text */ --music-player-text : var(--text-primary) ; --music-player-text-muted : var(--text-muted) ; /* Range track */ --music-player-track-bg : var(--border-color) ; --music-player-track-fill : var(--color-primary) ; /* Sizing */ --music-player-btn-size : 2.125rem ; --music-player-padding-x : 1.3125rem ; --music-player-padding-y : 0.8125rem ; } Options Reference Option Type Default Description tracks Array [] Array of { name, url } objects. name is displayed; url is the audio file path. volume number 0.5 Initial playback volume between 0 and 1. shuffle boolean false Shuffle the track list on init. Also shows the shuffle toggle button. showProgress boolean false Show a seek bar with elapsed/total time display. showPlaylist boolean false Show a collapsible track list panel. Also shows the shuffle button. autoAdvance boolean true Automatically play the next track when the current one ends. Bundled demo audio: Pale Blue Dot by Stellardrone from the album Invent the Universe . Source: Bandcamp . Licensed under CC BY 4.0 . No changes were made to the bundled audio file.

documents.title: Preloader
documents.bodyText: Striped:. Animated Striped:. Indeterminate:. Extra small ( .progress-xs ):. Small ( .progress-sm ):. Large ( .progress-lg ):. Extra large ( .progress-xl ):. Growing spinner:. Colored spinners:. The signature Vanduo Docs loader — four colored spinners arranged in a 2×2 grid with desynchronized animation. Used as the default loading indicator throughout the documentation.. Combine with skeleton cards for rich content placeholders. The spinner overlays the skeleton to indicate active loading.. <!-- Standalone Quad-Spinner Loader --> < div class = "vd-dynamic-loader" > < div class = "vd-dynamic-loader-grid" > < div class = "vd-spinner vd-spinner-sm vd-spinner-success" style = "animation-delay: 0s;" ></ div > < div class = "vd-spinner vd-spinner-sm vd-spinner-warning" style = "animation-delay: -0.15s;" ></ div > < div class = "vd-spinner vd-spinner-sm vd-spinner-error" style = "animation-delay: -0.3s;" ></ div > < div class = "vd-spinner vd-spinner-sm vd-spinner-info" style = "animation-delay: -0.45s;" ></ div > </ div > < span class = "vd-dynamic-loader-text" >Loading section...</ span > </ div > <!-- Required CSS (add to your stylesheet or <style> block) --> < style > .vd-dynamic-loader { display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; justify-content: center; padding: 4rem 1rem; gap: 1rem; } .vd-dynamic-loader-grid { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; gap: 0.5rem; } .vd-dynamic-loader-text { color: var(--text-muted); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); } </ style > <!-- Overlay on Skeleton Card (for lazy-loaded content) --> < div class = "vd-skeleton-card" style = "position: relative; min-height: 300px; padding: 2rem; overflow: hidden;" > < div class = "vd-skeleton vd-skeleton-heading-lg" ></ div > < div class = "vd-skeleton vd-skeleton-paragraph" > < div class = "vd-skeleton vd-skeleton-text" ></ div > < div class = "vd-skeleton vd-skeleton-text" ></ div > < div class = "vd-skeleton vd-skeleton-text" ></ div > </ div > < !-- Spinner overlay --> < div class = "vd-dynamic-loader" style = "position: absolute; inset: 0;" > < div class = "vd-dynamic-loader-grid" >...spinners...</ div > < span class = "vd-dynamic-loader-text" >Loading...</ span > </ div > </ div > Tip: The negative animation-delay values desynchronize each spinner, creating the signature "quad" effect. Adjust the delay values to change the visual rhythm.

documents.title: Search
documents.bodyText: A configurable client-side search component with keyboard navigation, debounced input, category grouping, and full accessibility support. Perfect for documentation sites, knowledge bases, or any content-heavy application.. Include the CSS and JS files, then add the search markup:. link rel = "stylesheet" href = "css/components/doc-search.css" > div class = "vd-doc-search" > div class = "vd-doc-search-input-wrapper" > i class = "ph ph-magnifying-glass vd-doc-search-icon" > i > input type = "search" class = "vd-doc-search-input" placeholder = "Search..." aria-label = "Search" aria-controls = "search-results" aria-expanded = "false" autocomplete = "off" > kbd class = "vd-doc-search-shortcut" >⌘K kbd > div > div class = "vd-doc-search-results" id = "search-results" role = "listbox" aria-label = "Search results" > div > div > script src = "js/components/doc-search.js" > script > The component auto-initializes on page load and indexes all elements with id attributes.. Create a custom search instance with your own data and callbacks:. // Create custom search instance const mySearch = Search. create ({ // Custom selectors inputSelector: '.my-search-input' , resultsSelector: '.my-search-results' , // Custom data source (optional) data: [ { title: 'Getting Started' , content: 'Learn how to install...' , category: 'Guides' , url: '#getting-started' }, // ... more items ], // Callbacks onSelect: (item) => { console. log ( 'Selected:' , item); window.location.hash = item.url; }, onSearch: (query, results) => { console. log ( `Found ${results.length} results` ); }, onOpen: () => console. log ( 'Search opened' ), onClose: () => console. log ( 'Search closed' ) }); CSS Classes Class Name Description Type .vd-doc-search Outer wrapper for the search component. Component .vd-doc-search-input-wrapper Container grouping the input, icon, and shortcut. Layout .vd-doc-search-input The actual text input element. Sub-component .vd-doc-search-icon Styling for the magnifying glass icon. Sub-component .vd-doc-search-shortcut Display badge for the keyboard shortcut. Sub-component .vd-doc-search-results Container for the autocomplete dropdown results. Sub-component Configuration Options Option Default Description inputSelector .doc-search-input Search input element selector resultsSelector .doc-search-results Results container selector data null Custom data array (auto-indexes DOM if null) debounceMs 150 Debounce delay in milliseconds maxResults 20 Maximum results to display minQueryLength 2 Minimum characters to trigger search JavaScript API View Code JavaScript Copy // Access the default instance const search = DocSearch; // Or create a custom instance const search = Search. create ({ /* options */ }); // Methods search. open (); // Open/focus search search. close (); // Close results search. clear (); // Clear input and results search. destroy (); // Remove all event listeners // Update search index (after DOM changes) search. reindex (); // Search programmatically search. search ( 'query' ); // Get current state const isOpen = search.isOpen; Category Icons Customize icons for each category:. const search = Search. create ({ categoryIcons: { 'Getting Started' : 'ph-rocket' , 'Components' : 'ph-cube' , 'API' : 'ph-code' , 'Guides' : 'ph-book-open' , default : 'ph-file-text' } }); Icons use Phosphor Icons classes. The default key is used for categories without a specific icon.. Override CSS variables to customize the appearance:. Keyboard Navigation: Arrow keys, Enter to select, Escape to close. Keyboard Shortcut: ⌘K (Mac) / Ctrl+K (Windows) to focus. Debounced Input: 150ms delay prevents excessive searches. Category Grouping: Results grouped by section with icons. Highlight Matching: Search terms highlighted in results. ARIA Accessible: Full screen reader support. Dark Mode: Automatic theme adaptation. Reduced Motion: Respects prefers-reduced-motion

documents.title: Draggable Components
documents.bodyText: Flexible and accessible drag-and-drop system supporting mouse, touch, and keyboard interactions. Elements can be dragged, dropped into zones, and reordered within containers.. Add .vd-draggable to any element to make it draggable:. < div class = "vd-draggable" data-draggable = "item-1" > < span class = "vd-draggable-handle" >☰</ span > Drag me around </ div > With Handle Use .vd-draggable-handle to create a visible grab handle:. < div class = "vd-draggable" > < span class = "vd-draggable-handle" > < i class = "ph ph-dots-six-vertical" ></ i > </ span > Item with icon handle </ div > Sortable Container Wrap items in .vd-draggable-container vd-draggable-container-vertical for keyboard-reorderable lists:. < div class = "vd-draggable-container vd-draggable-container-vertical" > < div class = "vd-draggable-item" data-draggable = "sort-1" > < span class = "vd-draggable-handle" >☰</ span > First item </ div > < div class = "vd-draggable-item" data-draggable = "sort-2" >...</ div > </ div > Drop Zone Use .vd-drop-zone to create areas that accept dragged items:. < div class = "vd-draggable" data-draggable = "item" > Drag me into the zone </ div > < div class = "vd-drop-zone" aria-label = "Drop items here" > </ div > Programmatic Control Use the JavaScript API to add or remove draggable at runtime:. // Make any element draggable const el = document.querySelector ( '#my-element' ); VanduoDraggable. makeDraggable (el, { data: 'custom-data' }); // Remove draggable behavior VanduoDraggable. removeDraggable (el); Disabled State Add .is-disabled to prevent interaction:. < div class = "vd-draggable is-disabled" > < span class = "vd-draggable-handle" >☰</ span > I can't be dragged </ div > Custom Styling Override CSS variables for a custom look:. < div class = "vd-draggable custom-drag" > Custom styled draggable </ div > .custom-drag { --draggable-bg : var ( --color-primary-alpha-5 ); --draggable-border-color : var ( --color-primary ); --draggable-hover-bg : var ( --color-primary-alpha-10 ); } Accessibility The draggable component is fully accessible with keyboard navigation and screen reader support.. All events bubble and include a detail object.. const el = document.querySelector ( '.vd-draggable' ); el. addEventListener ( 'draggable:start' , (e) => { console.log ( 'Drag started:' , e.detail.data); }); el. addEventListener ( 'draggable:end' , (e) => { console.log ( 'Drag ended at:' , e.detail.position); }); CSS Classes Class Name Description Type .vd-draggable Base class to make an independent element draggable. Component .vd-draggable-handle Optional inner element designated as the specific drag grip area. Sub-component .vd-draggable-container-vertical Layout container that manages vertical sorting of items. Layout .vd-draggable-item Individual sortable item within a -container-vertical . Sub-component .vd-drop-zone Target container area that accepts dropped .vd-draggable elements. Component .is-disabled State class to prevent dragging interactions. State CSS Variables Variable Default --draggable-bg var(--color-white) --draggable-border-color var(--border-color) --draggable-hover-bg var(--bg-secondary) --draggable-hover-border-color var(--color-primary) --draggable-dragging-bg var(--bg-secondary) --draggable-dragging-border-color var(--color-primary) --draggable-dragging-shadow var(--shadow-lg) --drop-zone-bg var(--color-primary-alpha-5) --drop-zone-border-color var(--color-primary-alpha-30) --draggable-padding 0.8125rem (Fibonacci 13px) --draggable-margin 0.5rem (Fibonacci 8px) JavaScript API Use the global VanduoDraggable object for programmatic control:. role="option" on draggable items. role="listbox" on containers. aria-grabbed toggles during drag. aria-roledescription="draggable item" for screen readers. aria-dropeffect="move" on drop zones. tabindex="0" added automatically

documents.title: Carousel / Flow
documents.bodyText: Horizontal slide transition. Swipe or use controls to navigate. Fully responsive and accessible. Fade Variant Cross-fade transition Smooth opacity blend Elegant transitions View Code HTML Copy <!-- Add .vd-flow-fade for crossfade --> < div class = "vd-flow vd-flow-fade" > < div class = "vd-flow-track" > < div class = "vd-flow-slide" >...</ div > < div class = "vd-flow-slide" >...</ div > </ div > < button class = "vd-flow-prev" >‹</ button > < button class = "vd-flow-next" >›</ button > </ div > Autoplay with Indicators Auto-advances every 4s Pauses on hover Click indicator to jump View Code HTML Copy <!-- Autoplay with indicators --> < div class = "vd-flow" data-vd-autoplay = "true" data-vd-interval = "4000" data-vd-loop = "true" > < div class = "vd-flow-track" > < div class = "vd-flow-slide" >...</ div > < div class = "vd-flow-slide" >...</ div > </ div > < button class = "vd-flow-prev" >...</ button > < button class = "vd-flow-next" >...</ button > < div class = "vd-flow-indicators" > < button aria-label = "Go to slide 1" ></ button > < button aria-label = "Go to slide 2" ></ button > </ div > </ div > Compact Size Compact slide A. Compact slide B. Great for feature showcases and landing pages. Increased padding and height for impact. Navigation buttons include aria-label attributes for screen readers. Indicator buttons use aria-label="Go to slide N" for identification. Active indicator receives aria-current="true" and .is-active class. Autoplay pauses on :hover and :focus-within to respect user interaction. Keyboard navigation: ArrowLeft / ArrowRight move between slides when focused. The carousel container uses role="region" and aria-roledescription="carousel". Each slide uses role="group" and aria-roledescription="slide"

documents.title: Expanding Cards
documents.bodyText: A horizontal strip of panels that expand on focus — one stays wide while the others stay slim. Click a panel to expand it; use ← → when a panel is focused.. Uses background images on each .vd-expanding-card via --vd-expanding-card-bg .. Set --vd-expanding-card-bg to a flat color and --vd-expanding-card-bg-default on each card so the icon circle matches.. Interaction and layout are adapted from the MIT-licensed CodePen “Expanding flex cards” by Zed Dash. Vanduo adds prefixed class names, documentation, optional keyboard navigation, and reduced-motion handling.. < div class = "vd-expanding-cards" data-vd-expanding-cards > < div class = "vd-expanding-card is-active" style = "--vd-expanding-card-bg: url(...);" > < div class = "vd-expanding-card-shadow" ></ div > < div class = "vd-expanding-card-label" > < div class = "vd-expanding-card-icon" >…</ div > < div class = "vd-expanding-card-info" > < div class = "vd-expanding-card-title" >Title</ div > < div class = "vd-expanding-card-subtitle" >Subtitle</ div > </ div > </ div > </ div > </ div > Class reference Class / attribute Description .vd-expanding-cards Flex row container. Initialized automatically unless data-vd-expanding-cards="manual" . .vd-expanding-card Single panel. Active panel has .is-active . --vd-expanding-card-bg Background image or color for the panel. --vd-expanding-card-bg-default Fallback color (nth-child presets or your own) — drives icon circle text color. .vd-expanding-card-shadow Bottom gradient scrim. .vd-expanding-card-label Icon + title row anchored to the corner. JS: VanduoExpandingCards.init() runs with Vanduo.init() . Use Vanduo.reinit('expandingCards') after injecting markup.. Attribution Interaction and layout are adapted from the MIT-licensed CodePen “Expanding flex cards” by Zed Dash. Vanduo adds prefixed class names, documentation, optional keyboard navigation, and reduced-motion handling.

documents.title: Spotlight / Feature Discovery
documents.bodyText: Guide users through your interface with step-by-step tours. An overlay highlights target elements while a tooltip provides context and navigation controls.. Click the button below to start a 3-step feature discovery tour that highlights elements inside this demo area.. const steps = [ { target: '#search-input' , title: 'Search' , description: 'Find anything fast.' }, { target: '.notifications-bell' , title: 'Notifications' , description: 'Your latest alerts.' } ]; // Start the tour VanduoSpotlight.start(steps); // Programmatic navigation VanduoSpotlight.next(); VanduoSpotlight.prev(); // End the tour early VanduoSpotlight.stop(); Declarative Usage Attach a JSON array of steps directly to a trigger element with data-vd-spotlight . After Vanduo.init() , clicking that trigger starts the tour automatically.. The tooltip uses role="dialog" and aria-modal="true" to indicate a modal context to assistive technologies.. Pressing Escape or clicking the overlay ends the tour, and focus returns to the trigger when possible.. The overlay is marked aria-hidden="true" so screen readers skip the backdrop.. Each step's title and description are connected to the tooltip with aria-labelledby and aria-describedby .. The footer includes an aria-label announcing the current step count.. Keep step copy concise and make sure every highlighted target remains understandable even if a user dismisses the tour early.

chunks.text: Guide users through your interface with step-by-step tours. An overlay highlights target elements while a tooltip provides context and navigation controls.

documents.title: Timeline
documents.bodyText: Display chronological events in a vertical track. By default the timeline is pure CSS — no JavaScript required. Supports alternating layout and color-coded markers for different event states.. Animated demos: Scroll to this section to watch each timeline reveal item-by-item. Add .vd-timeline-animated on .vd-timeline to opt in; the framework sets staggered --vd-timeline-reveal-delay per item (auto-init via Vanduo.init() ). With .vd-timeline-alternating , items animate in from the left and right on desktop (same stagger); on narrow viewports the motion stays vertical. Add .vd-timeline-playback together with .vd-timeline-animated to drive reveals from buttons ( data-vd-timeline-prev , data-vd-timeline-next , data-vd-timeline-play , data-vd-timeline-pause ) in the direct parent of the timeline (e.g. .vd-card-body wrapping both the toolbar and the list)—scroll-based IntersectionObserver is not used in playback mode.. Initial release of the framework with core components, grid system, and theme engine.. Community feedback collected, accessibility audit completed, and performance benchmarks run.. Design tokens defined, Figma components created, and documentation site scaffolded.. Requirements gathered, architecture decided, and technology stack finalized.. All 142 tests passed. Bundle size within budget.. The .vd-card-flat class will be removed in v3.0. Migrate to .vd-card-outlined .. CDN purge timed out. Rollback initiated automatically.. Team synced on sprint goals and blockers for the day.. Reviewed pull requests for the new grid system and responsive utilities.. Discussed token naming conventions and dark-mode color adjustments.. Version 2.4.0 published to npm and CDN. Changelog updated.. Goals and scope confirmed for the milestone.. Feature work merged behind a feature flag.. QA sign-off and docs updated before release.. Flag enabled for all users; monitoring dashboards green.. Use an <ol> or add role="list" on the container and role="listitem" on each .vd-timeline-item so events are announced as an ordered sequence.. Markers are decorative — apply aria-hidden="true" on .vd-timeline-marker icons.. Ensure .vd-timeline-date text uses a <time> element with a datetime attribute for machine-readable dates.. Color variants must not be the sole indicator of state — pair them with text labels or icons.. The alternating layout collapses to a single column on narrow viewports, maintaining natural reading order.. Default: CSS only. Static timelines need no JavaScript. With .vd-timeline-animated alone, the framework runs one shared IntersectionObserver (auto-init via Vanduo.init() ) to add .is-revealed . With .vd-timeline-playback , there is no observer—only the buttons. Use VanduoTimeline.reinit() after injecting new timeline markup.

chunks.text: Display chronological events in a vertical track. By default the timeline is pure CSS — no JavaScript required. Supports alternating layout and color-coded markers for different event states.
chunks.heading: Timeline

chunks.text: Animated demos: Scroll to this section to watch each timeline reveal item-by-item. Add .vd-timeline-animated on .vd-timeline to opt in; the framework sets staggered --vd-timeline-reveal-delay per item (auto-init via Vanduo.init() ). With .vd-timeline-alternating , items animate in from the left and right on desktop (same stagger); on narrow viewports the motion stays vertical. Add .vd-timeline-playback together with .vd-timeline-animated to drive reveals from buttons ( data-vd-timeline-prev , data-vd-timeline-next , data-vd-timeline-play , data-vd-timeline-pause ) in the direct parent of the timeline (e.g. .vd-card-body wrapping both the toolbar and the list)—scroll-based IntersectionObserver is not used in playback mode.
chunks.heading: Timeline

chunks.text: .vd-timeline — Base container. Draws a vertical line and positions items along it.
chunks.heading: CSS Classes

chunks.text: .vd-timeline-item — Individual event wrapper containing a marker and content block.
chunks.heading: CSS Classes

chunks.text: .vd-timeline-marker — Circular dot positioned on the timeline track. Default size is 12px.
chunks.heading: CSS Classes

chunks.text: .vd-timeline-marker-lg — Larger marker (24px) that can contain an icon.
chunks.heading: CSS Classes

chunks.text: .vd-timeline-content — Text block beside the marker holding title, date, and description.
chunks.heading: CSS Classes

chunks.text: .vd-timeline-title — Heading text for the event.
chunks.heading: CSS Classes

chunks.text: .vd-timeline-date — Small muted text for the event date or timestamp.
chunks.heading: CSS Classes

chunks.text: .vd-timeline-text — Body text describing the event in detail.
chunks.heading: CSS Classes

chunks.text: .vd-timeline-alternating — Modifier that alternates items left and right of the center line (desktop only).
chunks.heading: CSS Classes

chunks.text: .vd-timeline-animated — Optional modifier (use with .vd-timeline ) for fade/slide-in and marker pop-in. With scroll (default), items gain .is-revealed when scrolled into view. With .vd-timeline-playback , items are revealed only via controls (see below). Delays use --vd-timeline-reveal-delay (set per item by VanduoTimeline ).
chunks.heading: CSS Classes

chunks.text: .vd-timeline-playback — Use with .vd-timeline-animated . Disables scroll-driven reveal; place data-vd-timeline-prev , data-vd-timeline-next , data-vd-timeline-play , and data-vd-timeline-pause on buttons inside the timeline’s direct parent (e.g. toolbar and list share a .vd-card-body ). Cumulative reveal: next/back add or remove .is-revealed on items in order; play auto-advances on an interval until the end or pause.
chunks.heading: CSS Classes

chunks.text: --vd-timeline-reveal-delay — Per-item transition delay for the reveal animation (inline-set when using the animated modifier; first items stagger ~140ms apart, capped after the eighth).
chunks.heading: CSS Classes

chunks.text: .vd-timeline-success — Colors the marker with the success (green) palette.
chunks.heading: CSS Classes

chunks.text: .vd-timeline-warning — Colors the marker with the warning (amber) palette.
chunks.heading: CSS Classes

chunks.text: .vd-timeline-danger — Colors the marker with the danger (red) palette.
chunks.heading: CSS Classes

chunks.text: Use an <ol> or add role="list" on the container and role="listitem" on each .vd-timeline-item so events are announced as an ordered sequence.
chunks.heading: Accessibility

chunks.text: Markers are decorative — apply aria-hidden="true" on .vd-timeline-marker icons.
chunks.heading: Accessibility

chunks.text: Ensure .vd-timeline-date text uses a <time> element with a datetime attribute for machine-readable dates.
chunks.heading: Accessibility

chunks.text: Color variants must not be the sole indicator of state — pair them with text labels or icons.
chunks.heading: Accessibility

chunks.text: The alternating layout collapses to a single column on narrow viewports, maintaining natural reading order.
chunks.heading: Accessibility

documents.title: Popover / Bubble
documents.bodyText: Trigger elements receive aria-describedby pointing to the popover ID. The popover uses role="tooltip" for untitled and role="dialog" for titled variants. Close buttons include aria-label="Close" for screen readers. Popover is dismissed on Escape keypress. Focus is trapped inside titled popovers and returned to the trigger on close. Popover auto-repositions to stay within the viewport (collision detection)

documents.title: Offcanvas
documents.bodyText: Extend the Sidenav into a full offcanvas panel that slides in from any edge. Uses the existing VanduoSidenav API with positional modifiers.. Default offcanvas slides in from the left edge.. Add .vd-offcanvas-right or data-vd-position="right" .. Full-width panel sliding down from the top edge — great for search overlays or notification trays.. <!-- Top offcanvas panel --> < button data-sidenav-toggle = "#offcanvas-top" > Open Top </ button > < aside class = "vd-offcanvas vd-offcanvas-top" id = "offcanvas-top" data-vd-position = "top" > < div class = "vd-sidenav-header" > < h3 class = "vd-sidenav-title" >Top Panel</ h3 > < button class = "vd-sidenav-close" >×</ button > </ div > < div class = "vd-sidenav-body" >...</ div > </ aside > Bottom Open Bottom Bottom Panel × Full-width panel sliding up from the bottom — ideal for mobile action sheets.. <!-- Bottom offcanvas panel --> < aside class = "vd-offcanvas vd-offcanvas-bottom" data-vd-position = "bottom" > ... </ aside > Using data-vd-position You can set the slide direction entirely through the data-vd-position attribute instead of adding directional CSS classes. The JS reads this attribute at init time and applies the correct transform.. Offcanvas uses role="dialog" and aria-modal="true" when open. Close button includes aria-label="Close" for assistive technology. Focus is trapped within the panel while open and returned to the trigger on close. Pressing Escape closes the offcanvas panel. Overlay backdrop blocks interaction with the page beneath. The trigger button uses aria-expanded reflecting open/closed state

documents.title: FAB (Floating Action Button)
documents.bodyText: The circular FAB is designed for the single most important action on a screen. Use an icon that clearly represents the action.. Tip: Add .vd-fab-fixed to pin the FAB to the viewport corner. Without it, the FAB flows inline like a regular button.. <!-- Inline FAB (no fixed positioning) --> < button class = "vd-fab" aria-label = "Add" > < i class = "ph ph-plus" ></ i > </ button > <!-- Fixed to bottom-right of viewport --> < button class = "vd-fab vd-fab-fixed" aria-label = "Add" > < i class = "ph ph-plus" ></ i > </ button > Sizes Three sizes based on Fibonacci spacing. Use small for secondary actions, default for primary, and large for hero-level prominence.. < button class = "vd-fab vd-fab-sm" >...</ button > <!-- 40px --> < button class = "vd-fab" >...</ button > <!-- 56px (default) --> < button class = "vd-fab vd-fab-lg" >...</ button > <!-- 72px --> Extended FAB (with Label) When the icon alone isn't enough, use the extended variant to pair an icon with a text label. The pill shape keeps the elevated FAB feel while improving clarity.. < button class = "vd-fab vd-fab-extended" > < i class = "ph ph-plus" ></ i >Create </ button > < button class = "vd-fab vd-fab-extended vd-fab-success" > < i class = "ph ph-check" ></ i >Approve </ button > Color Variants Semantic colors communicate intent at a glance. Use the default primary for standard actions, success for confirmations, and danger for destructive operations.. < button class = "vd-fab" >...</ button > <!-- primary (default) --> < button class = "vd-fab vd-fab-secondary" >...</ button > <!-- secondary / gray --> < button class = "vd-fab vd-fab-success" >...</ button > <!-- success / green --> < button class = "vd-fab vd-fab-danger" >...</ button > <!-- danger / red --> Position Variants Position classes pin the FAB to a viewport corner with position: fixed . Each class is self-contained — no need to add .vd-fab-fixed separately.. <!-- Fixed to bottom-right (default) --> < button class = "vd-fab vd-fab-fixed" >...</ button > <!-- Other corners --> < button class = "vd-fab vd-fab-bottom-left" >...</ button > < button class = "vd-fab vd-fab-top-right" >...</ button > < button class = "vd-fab vd-fab-top-left" >...</ button > < button class = "vd-fab vd-fab-center" >...</ button > Speed Dial Menu Group related actions behind a single FAB trigger. Clicking the main button reveals a stack of smaller action buttons with a staggered entrance animation.. Click the FAB above to toggle the speed dial. Uses a simple .is-open class toggle and aria-expanded — no JS framework required.. <!-- Speed dial FAB menu --> < div class = "vd-fab-menu" > < button class = "vd-fab" aria-label = "Toggle menu" aria-expanded = "false" onclick = "this.parentElement.classList.toggle('is-open')" > < i class = "ph ph-plus" ></ i > </ button > < div class = "vd-fab-actions" > < button class = "vd-fab vd-fab-sm" aria-label = "Upload" >...</ button > < button class = "vd-fab vd-fab-sm" aria-label = "Photo" >...</ button > </ div > </ div > <!-- Fixed speed dial in bottom-right corner --> < div class = "vd-fab-menu vd-fab-fixed" >...</ div > When to Use a FAB Do Use for the single most important action on a screen (e.g. "New message", "Add item") Use .vd-fab-fixed or a position class to anchor it to the viewport Keep icon-only FABs to universally understood icons (plus, edit, share) Use the extended variant when the icon alone may be ambiguous Use the speed dial pattern to group 3–6 related actions Don't Don't place more than one fixed FAB on a page — it dilutes focus Don't use a FAB for minor or infrequent actions — use a regular .vd-btn Don't skip aria-label on icon-only FABs Don't use more than 6 actions in a speed dial — it becomes overwhelming API Reference CSS Classes Class Description Type .vd-fab Base floating action button — circular with elevation. Flows inline by default. Base .vd-fab-fixed Pins the FAB to the bottom-right corner of the viewport with position: fixed Position .vd-fab-sm Small FAB (40×40px) Size .vd-fab-lg Large FAB (72×72px) Size .vd-fab-extended Pill-shaped FAB with icon + text label Variant .vd-fab-bottom-left Fixed position: bottom-left corner of the viewport Position .vd-fab-top-right Fixed position: top-right corner of the viewport Position .vd-fab-top-left Fixed position: top-left corner of the viewport Position .vd-fab-center Fixed position: bottom-center of the viewport Position .vd-fab-secondary Secondary/gray colour variant Color .vd-fab-success Success/green colour variant Color .vd-fab-danger Danger/red colour variant Color .vd-fab-menu Container for a speed-dial group. Flows inline by default; add .vd-fab-fixed to anchor it. Structure .vd-fab-actions Wrapper for child action buttons inside a speed dial (animated on toggle) Structure .is-open Toggle class on .vd-fab-menu to reveal speed dial actions State JavaScript The FAB is a CSS-only component. Speed dial toggling uses a simple .is-open class toggle — no dedicated JS module is required. You can add your own click handler or use the inline onclick example above.. Use for the single most important action on a screen (e.g. "New message", "Add item"). Use .vd-fab-fixed or a position class to anchor it to the viewport. Keep icon-only FABs to universally understood icons (plus, edit, share). Use the extended variant when the icon alone may be ambiguous. Use the speed dial pattern to group 3–6 related actions. Don't place more than one fixed FAB on a page — it dilutes focus. Don't use a FAB for minor or infrequent actions — use a regular .vd-btn. Don't skip aria-label on icon-only FABs. Don't use more than 6 actions in a speed dial — it becomes overwhelming. Icon-only FABs require aria-label describing the action. Extended FABs with visible text labels do not need aria-label. Speed dial trigger should use aria-expanded reflecting menu state. Speed dial action container uses role="menu" and children use role="menuitem". Focus order follows the DOM: trigger first, then actions from top to bottom. FABs support standard :focus-visible outline for keyboard users

documents.title: Scrollspy / Waypoint
documents.bodyText: Waypoint automatically highlights navigation links as the user scrolls through corresponding content sections. It observes section visibility and updates the .is-active class on the matching nav link.. Add data-vd-waypoint-nav to the nav container and point it to the scrollable parent. Each link href should reference a section ID within the scroll container.. Waypoint supports vertical scroll containers of any height. Links are highlighted top-down - the topmost visible section wins. Works with both window scroll and overflow containers.. Call VanduoWaypoint.refresh(nav) after dynamic content changes. Destroy with VanduoWaypoint.destroy(nav) to clean up observers.. <!-- Sidebar scrollspy navigation --> < nav class = "vd-waypoint-nav" data-vd-waypoint-nav = "#scroll-container" > < a href = "#section-1" class = "is-active" >Section 1</ a > < a href = "#section-2" >Section 2</ a > < a href = "#section-3" >Section 3</ a > </ nav > < div id = "scroll-container" style = "overflow-y:auto;" > < div id = "section-1" >...</ div > < div id = "section-2" >...</ div > < div id = "section-3" >...</ div > </ div > Underline Variant Overview Features Pricing FAQ Overview of the current section with a compact underline indicator.. < nav class = "vd-waypoint-nav vd-waypoint-underline" data-vd-waypoint-nav = "window" > < a href = "#overview" class = "is-active" >Overview</ a > < a href = "#features" >Features</ a > < a href = "#pricing" >Pricing</ a > </ nav > Pill Variant Getting Started Components Utilities Theming Getting started stays selected while the card remains anchored in place.. Navigation container uses role="navigation" with aria-label="Table of contents". Active link receives aria-current="true" in addition to the .is-active class. Links use standard anchor elements for native keyboard navigation and focus management. Smooth scrolling respects prefers-reduced-motion media query. Section targets use semantic heading hierarchy for screen reader landmark navigation

chunks.text: Waypoint automatically highlights navigation links as the user scrolls through corresponding content sections. It observes section visibility and updates the .is-active class on the matching nav link.
chunks.heading: Introduction

chunks.text: Add data-vd-waypoint-nav to the nav container and point it to the scrollable parent. Each link href should reference a section ID within the scroll container.
chunks.heading: Setup

chunks.text: Waypoint supports vertical scroll containers of any height. Links are highlighted top-down - the topmost visible section wins. Works with both window scroll and overflow containers.
chunks.heading: Usage

chunks.text: Call VanduoWaypoint.refresh(nav) after dynamic content changes. Destroy with VanduoWaypoint.destroy(nav) to clean up observers.
chunks.heading: API

chunks.text: Overview of the current section with a compact underline indicator.
chunks.heading: Underline Variant

chunks.text: Getting started stays selected while the card remains anchored in place.
chunks.heading: Pill Variant

Scroll down inside this container to see the sticky bar pin itself. The component automatically uses this panel as its scroll context.. Place content before the affix element so it can scroll naturally into position, then let VanduoAffix pin it within the nearest scrollable parent.. documents.title: Stepper
documents.bodyText: Multi-step progress indicators for wizards, checkout flows, and onboarding sequences. Supports horizontal, vertical, and clickable variants with built-in state management.. Click any step to navigate directly. The error state on step 3 shows validation feedback.. The stepper container uses role="list" and each step uses role="listitem" for assistive technology.. Active step is announced via aria-current="step" .. Completed steps convey state through aria-label="Completed" on the circle.. Error steps include aria-label="Error" for screen reader feedback.. Clickable steppers add tabindex="0" and respond to Enter and Space key presses.. Focus order follows the visual step sequence from first to last.

documents.title: Autocomplete / Suggest
documents.bodyText: The VanduoSuggest component adds type-ahead suggestions to any text input. Feed it a static JSON array or point it at a remote JSON endpoint — results filter as the user types. Alias: data-vd-autocomplete .. The input receives role="combobox" and aria-autocomplete="list". The suggestion list has role="listbox" ; each item has role="option". aria-expanded toggles true / false as the dropdown opens and closes. aria-activedescendant tracks the currently highlighted option for screen readers. ↑ / ↓ arrow keys navigate the list; Enter selects; Escape closes. Focus returns to the input after selection, preserving natural tab order

documents.title: Form Validation
documents.bodyText: The VanduoValidate component provides declarative, attribute-driven form validation. Chain rules with pipe syntax ( required|email|min:3 ), choose when validation triggers, and customise every error message — all without writing JavaScript.. Invalid fields receive aria-invalid="true" ; valid fields get aria-invalid="false". Error messages are linked via aria-describedby so screen readers announce them. Error elements use role="alert" for immediate announcement on live changes. Native <form> semantics preserved — novalidate disables browser UI, not semantics. Focus moves to the first invalid field on submit for keyboard users

documents.title: Date Picker
documents.bodyText: The VanduoDatepicker component attaches a calendar popup to any text input. Supports custom date formats, min/max date constraints, and month/year navigation — all driven by data attributes.. Minimal setup: add data-vd-datepicker to a text input (these demos use readonly so typing is off). With no format attribute, picked dates are written as YYYY-MM-DD . Focus the field to open the calendar and choose a day.. < input type = "text" class = "vd-input" placeholder = "YYYY-MM-DD" data-vd-datepicker readonly > Pre-selected Value & Custom Format Beyond basic: set value and data-vd-datepicker-format so the initial string is parsed and new picks match that pattern. Tokens: YYYY , MM , DD (case-insensitive).. < input type = "text" class = "vd-input" value = "01/15/2026" data-vd-datepicker data-vd-datepicker-format = "MM/DD/YYYY" readonly > Min / Max Date Constraints Use data-vd-datepicker-min and data-vd-datepicker-max with YYYY-MM-DD bounds. Out-of-range days are disabled; the view snaps to a month that contains selectable dates.. < input type = "text" class = "vd-input" data-vd-datepicker data-vd-datepicker-min = "2026-05-01" data-vd-datepicker-max = "2026-05-31" readonly > Month / Year Navigation This card uses DD.MM.YYYY to show a non-default format; navigation is the same as everywhere. Click the month/year title to switch day → month → year (decade) views; arrows step by month, year, or decade depending on the level.. The popup has role="dialog" and aria-label="Choose date". Calendar uses role="grid" ; weekday labels use role="columnheader" ; in-month days use role="gridcell". ← / → move by day; ↑ / ↓ move by week. Home / End jump to start/end of the current week. Page Up / Page Down navigate by month. Enter selects the focused date; Escape closes the popup and returns focus to the input. Disabled dates have aria-disabled="true" and are skipped during keyboard navigation

documents.title: Time Picker
documents.bodyText: The VanduoTimepicker component adds a scrollable time-selection dropdown to any text input. Choose between 12-hour and 24-hour formats, and configure the minute step interval.. The dropdown has role="listbox" ; each time slot has role="option". The input receives aria-haspopup="listbox" and aria-expanded state. ↑ / ↓ arrow keys navigate time slots; Enter selects; Escape closes. aria-activedescendant tracks the focused time slot for screen readers. Selected slot receives aria-selected="true". Focus returns to the input after selection or dismissal

documents.title: Rating
documents.bodyText: The VanduoRating component renders an interactive star-rating widget. Configure the maximum number of stars, pre-set a value, make it read-only, or pick a size variant — all via data attributes.. < div data-vd-rating ></ div > Pre-set Value (3 of 5) View Code HTML Copy < div data-vd-rating data-vd-rating-value = "3" ></ div > Read-Only User cannot change the value. Useful for displaying average scores.. < div data-vd-rating data-vd-rating-value = "4" data-vd-rating-readonly > </ div > Custom Max (10 Stars) View Code HTML Copy < div data-vd-rating data-vd-rating-max = "10" data-vd-rating-value = "7" > </ div > Size Variants Small. Default. Large. The container has role="radiogroup" with an aria-label like "Rating: 3 out of 5". Each star has role="radio" with aria-checked and aria-label (e.g. "3 stars"). ← / → arrow keys change the value; Home sets to 1; End sets to max. Read-only ratings use aria-readonly="true" and are not focusable. The widget is fully operable without a mouse via Tab + arrow keys

documents.title: Transfer / Multi-select
documents.bodyText: The VanduoTransfer component renders a dual-panel picker — users move items from a "Source" list to a "Target" list using arrow buttons. Search filtering is built into both panels automatically, and selected items are accessible programmatically at any time.. Each panel list has role="listbox" with aria-multiselectable="true". Items use role="option" with aria-selected reflecting checked state. Move buttons have descriptive aria-label s (e.g. "Move selected to right"). Space toggles item selection; ↑ / ↓ navigate the list. Ctrl+A selects all visible items in the focused panel. Search inputs are labelled with aria-label="Filter available items" / "Filter selected items". Panel headers use aria-live="polite" to announce count changes

documents.title: Tree View
documents.bodyText: The VanduoTree component renders hierarchical data as an expandable/collapsible tree. Supports checkbox selection with parent-child cascade, connection lines, and initial open state — all configured via a single JSON data attribute.. < div data-vd-tree = '[ { "id": "src", "label": "src", "icon": "ph ph-folder", "children": [ { "id": "btn", "label": "Button.vue", "icon": "ph ph-file-vue" }, { "id": "card", "label": "Card.vue", "icon": "ph ph-file-vue" } ] }, { "id": "pkg", "label": "package.json", "icon": "ph ph-file" } ]' ></ div > Checkbox Tree with Cascade View Code HTML JS Copy < div data-vd-tree = '[ { "id": "permissions", "label": "Permissions", "children": [ { "id": "users", "label": "User Management", "children": [ { "id": "users-read", "label": "View Users" }, { "id": "users-create", "label": "Create Users" } ] } ] } ]' data-vd-tree-checkbox data-vd-tree-cascade > </ div > const tree = document.querySelector( '[data-vd-tree-checkbox]' ); // Get all checked node IDs const checked = VanduoTree.getChecked(tree); console.log(checked); // ["users-read", "users-create"] // Listen for check changes tree.addEventListener( 'tree:check' , (e) => { console.log( 'Checked nodes:' , e.detail.checked); }); Initially Open Nodes Set "open": true on any node in the JSON data to expand it on render.. < div data-vd-tree = '[ { "id": "docs", "label": "Documentation", "open": true, "children": [ { "id": "getting-started", "label": "Getting Started", "open": true, "children": [ { "id": "install", "label": "Installation" }, { "id": "quick-start", "label": "Quick Start" } ] } ] } ]' ></ div > Generated HTML Structure The component generates the following DOM structure from the JSON data. You can style each part using the CSS classes listed in the API reference.. The root container has role="tree" ; each node has role="treeitem". Child groups are wrapped in role="group". Branch nodes set aria-expanded="true" or "false" to reflect open state. ↑ / ↓ navigate between visible nodes; → expands a branch; ← collapses it. Home jumps to the first node; End jumps to the last visible node. Space toggles the checkbox on checkbox trees. Enter activates (selects/expands) the focused node. Type-ahead: pressing a letter key jumps to the next node whose label starts with that character

documents.title: Glass Effects
documents.bodyText: Vanduo glass effects provide an opt-in frosted surface system built with backdrop-filter , translucent layers, highlight sheen, and accessibility fallbacks.. Use .vd-glass for frosted surfaces over rich backgrounds, then add size and behavior modifiers as needed.. Why Distinctions Can Look Subtle Blur and opacity are context-dependent. Smooth or homogeneous backgrounds can hide what each class changes.. Switch backdrop modes and force floating hover state to inspect class differences under the same conditions.. Look for sharper background detail and a lighter frosted layer.. Equivalent to .vd-glass default; use it for explicit readability.. Background texture softens while color bleed feels denser.. Strongest frosted depth; best for hero overlays and modal foregrounds.. Modifiers .vd-glass-tinted Adds tint gradient overlay Base glass Neutral translucent layer Tinted glass Adds primary tone wash Watch for color cast shift while blur and depth remain similar.. State-driven modifier; turn on Force floating hover to preview motion cue.. Use on busy imagery where stronger edge and body contrast improves readability.. Glass Tokens Token Purpose Default --vd-glass-blur Backdrop blur amount 12px --vd-glass-saturate Backdrop saturation boost 1.8 --vd-glass-bg-opacity Glass opacity base 0.65 --vd-glass-bg-light Light translucent surface rgba(255,255,255,var(--vd-glass-bg-opacity)) --vd-glass-bg-dark Dark translucent surface rgba(30,30,30,var(--vd-glass-bg-opacity)) --vd-glass-border-light Glass edge stroke rgba(255,255,255,var(--vd-glass-border-alpha)) --vd-glass-highlight-alpha Specular sheen intensity 0.25 --vd-glass-shadow Depth shadow for floating glass 0 8px 32px rgba(0,0,0,0.12) --vd-glass-noise-opacity Noise grain overlay strength 0.03 Component Integration Vanduo Glass Docs Card Glass Apply .vd-card-glass for elevated frosted cards. Toast Glass Use .vd-toast-glass for floating notifications. Open Glass Modal Modal Glass × The .vd-modal-glass variant applies frosted glass styling to modal surfaces. Close Confirm View Code HTML Copy Accessibility Glass classes include prefers-reduced-transparency: reduce fallbacks that disable blur and return to solid surfaces. Floating interactions respect prefers-reduced-motion: reduce . Use .vd-glass-contrast on busy backgrounds to improve text legibility. Keep body text contrast above WCAG AA by testing overlays in both light and dark themes. Theming Override glass tokens per app, theme, or component scope.. :root { --vd-glass-blur : 14px ; --vd-glass-bg-opacity : 0.7 ; --vd-glass-shadow : 0 14px 40px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.2) ; } .marketing-hero .vd-glass { --vd-glass-tint : rgba(34, 184, 207, 0.18) ; } Scroll-Activated Glass Two complementary APIs let you activate glass effects on scroll rather than at page-load time. The framework handles all the observer/listener logic — no custom JavaScript required.. Add .vd-navbar-glass to any .vd-navbar-fixed or .vd-navbar-sticky . The navbar starts fully transparent and gains the frosted glass surface once the user scrolls past its height (or a custom threshold set with data-scroll-threshold ). The .vd-navbar-scrolled class is toggled automatically by the framework.. <nav class = "vd-navbar vd-navbar-fixed vd-navbar-glass" > ... </nav> <!-- Custom scroll threshold (px) --> <nav class = "vd-navbar vd-navbar-fixed vd-navbar-glass" data-scroll-threshold = "120" > ... </nav> The docs navbar above is a live example — scroll this page to see the effect.. Any element — sidebar, floating panel, sticky header — can adopt scroll-aware glass by pairing .vd-glass with the data-glass-scroll attribute. The framework uses IntersectionObserver to watch a sentinel element (previous sibling by default, or a CSS selector via data-glass-sentinel ) and toggles .is-glass-active automatically.. Scroll inside this box to see the glass surface activate once the sentinel element above leaves the viewport.. .vd-glass-md matches base .vd-glass , so they are intentionally equivalent.. .vd-glass-floating is primarily an interaction state, so static examples can under-sell it.. Use textured backdrops and side-by-side comparisons to make blur, tint, and contrast differences obvious.. Glass. Docs. Glass classes include prefers-reduced-transparency: reduce fallbacks that disable blur and return to solid surfaces.. Floating interactions respect prefers-reduced-motion: reduce .. Use .vd-glass-contrast on busy backgrounds to improve text legibility.. Keep body text contrast above WCAG AA by testing overlays in both light and dark themes.. No JavaScript required in your markup. Works on any scrollable container, not just the window. CSS handles the inactive→active transition via transition. Falls back to always-active when no sentinel is found

chunks.text: Vanduo glass effects provide an opt-in frosted surface system built with backdrop-filter , translucent layers, highlight sheen, and accessibility fallbacks.
chunks.heading: Glass Effects

chunks.text: Use .vd-glass for frosted surfaces over rich backgrounds, then add size and behavior modifiers as needed.
chunks.heading: GlassSurface

chunks.text: Blur and opacity are context-dependent. Smooth or homogeneous backgrounds can hide what each class changes.
chunks.heading: Why Distinctions Can Look Subtle

chunks.text: .vd-glass-md matches base .vd-glass , so they are intentionally equivalent.
chunks.heading: Why Distinctions Can Look Subtle

chunks.heading: Why Distinctions Can Look Subtle

chunks.text: Use textured backdrops and side-by-side comparisons to make blur, tint, and contrast differences obvious.

documents.title: Ripple / Waves
documents.bodyText: <!-- Add .vd-ripple to any clickable element --> < button class = "vd-btn vd-btn-primary vd-ripple" > Primary Ripple </ button > <!-- Or use the data attribute --> < button class = "vd-btn vd-btn-primary" data-vd-ripple > Primary Ripple </ button > Color Variants Default (light) Dark Ripple Primary Ripple View Code HTML Copy <!-- Default light ripple --> < button class = "vd-btn vd-ripple" >Default</ button > <!-- Dark ripple (for light backgrounds) --> < button class = "vd-btn vd-ripple vd-ripple-dark" >Dark</ button > <!-- Primary-coloured ripple --> < button class = "vd-btn vd-ripple vd-ripple-primary" >Primary</ button > <!-- Light ripple (for dark backgrounds) --> < button class = "vd-btn vd-ripple vd-ripple-light" >Light</ button > Ripple on Non-button Elements Click anywhere on this div. Ripple works on any element — not just buttons. The ripple is a purely visual effect and does not affect DOM focus or screen reader output. The generated .vd-ripple-wave element uses aria-hidden="true". Ripple animation respects prefers-reduced-motion: reduce — the wave is instant rather than animated. Non-button elements with ripple should include appropriate role and tabindex for keyboard access. Colour contrast of the ripple wave meets WCAG guidelines against all standard button backgrounds

documents.title: Water Morph
documents.bodyText: A liquid wave content-swap effect. Click triggers an expanding radial wave from the pointer position while the old content dissolves and the new content emerges. Works on any element.. Click to morph between two labeled states. The wave originates from your pointer.. < button class = "vd-morph" data-vd-morph > < span class = "vd-morph-content vd-morph-current" > < i class = "ph ph-sun" ></ i > Light Mode </ span > < span class = "vd-morph-content vd-morph-next" > < i class = "ph ph-moon" ></ i > Dark Mode </ span > </ button > Theme Preview Card Morph works on any container. This card-sized element swaps its entire surface.. < div class = "vd-morph theme-card" data-vd-morph > < span class = "vd-morph-content vd-morph-current" > < i class = "ph ph-sun" ></ i > < span >Light Theme</ span > </ span > < span class = "vd-morph-content vd-morph-next" > < i class = "ph ph-moon-stars" ></ i > < span >Dark Theme</ span > </ span > </ div > Status Badge Morph Click any badge to cycle. Each state overrides --morph-wave-color for a matching wave.. <!-- Override --morph-wave-color per status --> < span class = "vd-morph vd-morph-sm badge-online" data-vd-morph > < span class = "vd-morph-content vd-morph-current" > < i class = "ph ph-wifi-high" ></ i > Online </ span > < span class = "vd-morph-content vd-morph-next" > < i class = "ph ph-coffee" ></ i > Away </ span > </ span > <!-- CSS: .badge-online { --morph-wave-color: var(--color-success); } --> Caption Reveal Morph text-heavy content with .vd-morph-lg for a slower, dramatic effect.. Wave and shine layers use aria-hidden="true" and carry no semantic content. Animation fully respects prefers-reduced-motion: reduce — content swaps instantly with no wave. Non-button morph elements should include role and tabindex for keyboard access. The component works with Enter and Space keys on natively focusable elements

documents.title: Quick Start
documents.bodyText: The quickest way to get started — no install, no build step. Add two lines to any HTML file:. <!-- CSS --> < link rel = "stylesheet" href = "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/vanduo-oss/framework@v1.3.8/dist/vanduo.min.css" > <!-- JS --> < script src = "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/vanduo-oss/framework@v1.3.8/dist/vanduo.min.js" ></ script > < script >Vanduo. init ();</ script > Tip: Pin to a specific version for production: @v1.3.8 instead of @main. Download the dist/ folder and include locally — no internet connection required at runtime:. <!-- CSS --> < link rel = "stylesheet" href = "dist/vanduo.min.css" > <!-- JS --> < script src = "dist/vanduo.min.js" ></ script > < script >Vanduo. init ();</ script > The dist/ folder is self-contained with CSS, JS, fonts, and icons.. For development or modular imports:. <!-- Full framework --> < link rel = "stylesheet" href = "css/vanduo.css" > < script src = "js/vanduo.js" ></ script > < script >Vanduo. init ();</ script > <!-- Or pick individual modules --> < link rel = "stylesheet" href = "css/core/colors.css" > < link rel = "stylesheet" href = "css/components/buttons.css" > With a Bundler (Vite) Requires a bundler. The imports below use bare module specifiers ( @vanduo-oss/framework ) which browsers cannot resolve on their own. You must use a build tool like Vite . For static HTML files, use the CDN or Download options above. Step 1. Scaffold a Vite project and add Vanduo:. pnpm create vite my-app --template vanilla cd my-app pnpm add @vanduo-oss/framework Step 2. Import in your entry file (e.g. main.js ):. import '@vanduo-oss/framework/css' ; import { Vanduo } from '@vanduo-oss/framework' ; Vanduo. init (); Why pnpm? pnpm enforces a strict lockfile and creates an isolated node_modules structure by default. Vanduo's .npmrc security policies — like blocking exotic sub-dependencies and strict peer enforcement — work best with pnpm out of the box. Alternatives: npm install @vanduo-oss/framework and yarn add @vanduo-oss/framework will also work, but they do not enforce the same strict lockfile and isolated node_modules security guarantees that pnpm provides.. Override CSS variables in your own stylesheet to customize the entire framework:. :root { /* Brand colors */ --color-primary : #6366f1 ; --color-primary-dark : #4f46e5 ; --color-primary-light : #818cf8 ; /* Typography */ --font-family-base : 'Inter', sans-serif ; --font-size-base : 1 rem ; /* Spacing (Fibonacci-based) */ --spacing-1 : 1 px ; --spacing-2 : 2 px ; --spacing-3 : 3 px ; --spacing-5 : 5 px ; --spacing-8 : 8 px ; --spacing-13 : 13 px ; /* Borders */ --border-color : #e2e8f0 ; --btn-border-radius : 8 px ; } Theme Switching Vanduo supports light, dark, and system themes. Preference is stored in localStorage .. <!-- Select-based toggle --> < select data-toggle = "theme" > < option value = "system" >System</ option > < option value = "light" >Light</ option > < option value = "dark" >Dark</ option > </ select > <!-- Or button toggle (cycles modes) --> < button data-toggle = "theme" >Toggle</ button > How it works:. JavaScript components are configured via data-* attributes:. All components register with the global Vanduo object:. // Re-initialize a component after DOM changes Vanduo. reinit ( 'dropdown' ); // Destroy all component instances (cleanup) Vanduo. destroyAll (); // Register a custom component Vanduo. register ( 'myComponent' , { init : function () { /* ... */ } }); // Show a toast notification Vanduo.components.toast. show ({ title: 'Success' , message: 'Item saved.' , type: 'success' }); CSS State Classes:. Build your first complete page step by step — navbar, hero, cards, and footer in about 60 lines of HTML.. Understand which JavaScript module format is right for your project and how to set it up.. data-toggle="theme" binds the element. Preference saved to vanduo-theme-preference key. Sets [data-theme="dark"] on <html>. System mode removes the attribute, deferring to prefers-color-scheme. .is-open — open state (modals, dropdowns, sidenav). .is-active — active state (tabs, accordion items). .disabled — disabled state. Requires a bundler. The imports below use bare module specifiers ( @vanduo-oss/framework ) which browsers cannot resolve on their own. You must use a build tool like Vite . For static HTML files, use the CDN or Download options above.. Why pnpm? pnpm enforces a strict lockfile and creates an isolated node_modules structure by default. Vanduo's .npmrc security policies — like blocking exotic sub-dependencies and strict peer enforcement — work best with pnpm out of the box.

documents.title: Getting Started from Zero
headings: Step 1 of 7 Create Your Project Folder
Step 2 of 7 Add Vanduo to Your Page
Step 4 of 7 Add a Hero Section
Step 7 of 7 Open in Your Browser
documents.bodyText: Welcome! This guide walks you through building your very first web page with Vanduo — from an empty folder to a working, styled site. No prior framework experience needed. Think of this as a senior developer sitting next to you, walking you through every step.. By the end of this guide, you'll have a complete page with:. All of this in about 60 lines of HTML — no CSS to write, no JavaScript to configure.. Open your terminal and create a new folder for your project. This is where all your files will live:. mkdir my-first-vanduo-site cd my-first-vanduo-site Now create an index.html file. You can use any text editor — VS Code, Sublime Text, or even Notepad:. touch index.html Pro tip: If you're using VS Code, you can open the folder directly with code . after creating it. VS Code's Live Server extension is great for auto-refreshing as you edit! Step 2 of 7 Add Vanduo to Your Page The fastest way to get started is via CDN — no downloads, no installs, just two lines in your HTML. Open index.html and paste this starter template:. <!DOCTYPE html> < html lang = "en" data-theme = "light" > < head > < meta charset = "UTF-8" > < meta name = "viewport" content = "width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0" > < title >My First Vanduo Site</ title > <!-- Vanduo CSS — this gives you the entire design system --> < link rel = "stylesheet" href = "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/vanduo-oss/framework@v1.3.8/dist/vanduo.min.css" > </ head > < body > <!-- Your content will go here --> <!-- Vanduo JS — enables interactive components --> < script src = "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/vanduo-oss/framework@v1.3.8/dist/vanduo.min.js" ></ script > < script >Vanduo.init();</ script > </ body > </ html > Pro tip: For production, pin to a specific version like @v1.3.8 instead of @main so your site doesn't unexpectedly change when the framework updates. Why CDN? CDN (Content Delivery Network) hosts the files for you on fast servers worldwide. Your visitors download Vanduo from the nearest server — no setup on your end. Perfect for learning and prototyping. Building a real project? For production apps we recommend installing via pnpm add @vanduo-oss/framework instead. pnpm provides strict lockfile security and isolated node_modules that pair perfectly with Vanduo's .npmrc policies. See the Quick Start page for full package manager setup. Step 3 of 7 Add a Navbar Replace the <!-- Your content will go here --> comment with a navbar. This gives you a responsive navigation bar that collapses on mobile:. < nav class = "vd-navbar" > < div class = "vd-navbar-container" > < a href = "#" class = "vd-navbar-brand" >My Site</ a > < button class = "vd-navbar-toggle" aria-expanded = "false" aria-label = "Toggle navigation" > < span ></ span >< span ></ span >< span ></ span > </ button > < div class = "vd-navbar-menu" aria-hidden = "true" > < ul class = "vd-navbar-nav" > < li >< a href = "#" class = "vd-nav-link" >Home</ a ></ li > < li >< a href = "#" class = "vd-nav-link" >About</ a ></ li > < li >< a href = "#" class = "vd-nav-link" >Contact</ a ></ li > </ ul > </ div > </ div > </ nav > What's happening here:. Below the </nav> tag, add a hero section — the big, eye-catching area at the top of your page:. < section class = "vd-container vd-py-13" > < div class = "vd-row" > < div class = "vd-col-12 vd-col-md-8" > < h1 >Welcome to My Site</ h1 > < p class = "vd-text-lg vd-text-muted vd-mb-8" > This is my first website built with the Vanduo framework. It looks great and I didn't write a single line of CSS! </ p > < a href = "#" class = "vd-btn vd-btn-primary vd-btn-lg" > < i class = "ph ph-rocket-launch" ></ i > Get Started </ a > </ div > </ div > </ section > Why vd-col-md-8 ? This means "take up 8 out of 12 columns on medium screens and above." On mobile, it stretches full width ( vd-col-12 ). This keeps your text readable — nobody likes lines that stretch across the entire screen! Step 5 of 7 Add Content Cards Below the hero, let's add a grid of cards — a common pattern for showcasing features, blog posts, or services:. < section class = "vd-container vd-py-8" > < h2 class = "vd-mb-8" >Features</ h2 > < div class = "vd-row" > < div class = "vd-col-12 vd-col-md-4 vd-mb-5" > < div class = "vd-card" > < div class = "vd-card-body" > < h3 >< i class = "ph ph-lightning" ></ i > Fast</ h3 > < p >Lightweight CSS with no heavy dependencies.</ p > </ div > </ div > </ div > < div class = "vd-col-12 vd-col-md-4 vd-mb-5" > < div class = "vd-card" > < div class = "vd-card-body" > < h3 >< i class = "ph ph-devices" ></ i > Responsive</ h3 > < p >Looks great on phones, tablets, and desktops.</ p > </ div > </ div > </ div > < div class = "vd-col-12 vd-col-md-4 vd-mb-5" > < div class = "vd-card" > < div class = "vd-card-body" > < h3 >< i class = "ph ph-moon" ></ i > Dark Mode</ h3 > < p >Built-in light and dark theme support.</ p > </ div > </ div > </ div > </ div > </ section > The grid in action: Each card uses vd-col-md-4 (4 out of 12 = one-third width), so you get 3 columns on desktop. On mobile they stack vertically because of vd-col-12 .. Every site needs a footer. Add this before the closing </body> tag (but above the <script> tags):. < footer class = "vd-footer" > < div class = "vd-container" > < div class = "vd-row" > < div class = "vd-col-12 vd-col-md-6" > < p >© 2026 My Site. Built with Vanduo.</ p > </ div > < div class = "vd-col-12 vd-col-md-6" style = "text-align: right;" > < a href = "#" >Privacy</ a > · < a href = "#" >Terms</ a > </ div > </ div > </ div > </ footer > Step 7 of 7 Open in Your Browser Save your index.html and open it in a web browser. You can simply double-click the file, or use a local server:. # Option A: Just double-click index.html in your file manager # Option B: Use a simple HTTP server (recommended) npx http-server . -p 3000 # Then open http://localhost:3000 If you see a styled page with a navbar, hero, cards, and footer — you did it! You've built your first Vanduo site. The navbar collapses on mobile, the cards stack responsively, and everything has consistent typography and spacing. Bonus: Add a Theme Toggle Want dark mode? Just add a button anywhere in your page (the navbar is a great spot):. < button data-toggle = "theme" aria-label = "Toggle theme" > < i class = "ph ph-moon" ></ i > </ button > That's it — one attribute. Vanduo's JS handles the rest: toggling data-theme on the <html> element, saving the preference to localStorage , and updating all colors automatically.. Here's everything together — a complete, working page you can copy and paste:. <!DOCTYPE html> < html lang = "en" data-theme = "light" > < head > < meta charset = "UTF-8" > < meta name = "viewport" content = "width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0" > < title >My First Vanduo Site</ title > < link rel = "stylesheet" href = "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/vanduo-oss/framework@v1.3.8/dist/vanduo.min.css" > </ head > < body > < nav class = "vd-navbar" > < div class = "vd-navbar-container" > < a href = "#" class = "vd-navbar-brand" >My Site</ a > < button class = "vd-navbar-toggle" aria-expanded = "false" aria-label = "Toggle navigation" > < span ></ span >< span ></ span >< span ></ span > </ button > < div class = "vd-navbar-menu" aria-hidden = "true" > < ul class = "vd-navbar-nav" > < li >< a href = "#" class = "vd-nav-link" >Home</ a ></ li > < li >< a href = "#" class = "vd-nav-link" >About</ a ></ li > < li >< a href = "#" class = "vd-nav-link" >Contact</ a ></ li > </ ul > < button data-toggle = "theme" aria-label = "Toggle theme" style = "margin-left: auto;" > < i class = "ph ph-moon" ></ i > </ button > </ div > </ div > </ nav > < section class = "vd-container vd-py-13" > < div class = "vd-row" > < div class = "vd-col-12 vd-col-md-8" > < h1 >Welcome to My Site</ h1 > < p class = "vd-text-lg vd-text-muted vd-mb-8" > Built with the Vanduo framework — no custom CSS needed. </ p > < a href = "#" class = "vd-btn vd-btn-primary vd-btn-lg" > < i class = "ph ph-rocket-launch" ></ i > Get Started </ a

documents.title: Framework Integration
documents.bodyText: Vanduo is designed to be completely framework-agnostic. Because it is built with pure CSS and Vanilla JavaScript modules, it can be dropped into any modern frontend framework (React, Vue, Nuxt, Svelte, etc.) seamlessly.. When integrating Vanduo into a component-heavy framework, remember these two rules:. Vanduo's stylesheet should be imported once at the top level of your application. The exact file depends on your framework:. In a single-page application, navigating between routes replaces DOM elements without a full page reload. Vanduo's event listeners are attached to specific elements, so you need to re-initialize after each route change.. The pattern is the same in every framework: call Vanduo.init() in the mounting lifecycle of each page component that uses Vanduo interactive features (modals, dropdowns, tabs, tooltips, etc.).. Static content (grids, cards, typography, utilities) works without init() — only interactive JavaScript components need it.. When using Server-Side Rendering (Next.js, Nuxt, SvelteKit), Vanduo's CSS works perfectly — it's just a stylesheet and renders on the server without issues.. However, Vanduo's JavaScript requires the window and document APIs, which don't exist on the server. Follow these rules:. 'use client'; import { useEffect } from 'react'; import { Vanduo } from 'vanduo'; export default function VanduoProvider({ children }) { useEffect(() => { Vanduo.init(); return () => Vanduo.destroy(); }, []); return <>{children}</>; } What's Next? Building Your First Layout Ready to build? Learn how the grid system works to structure your pages.. Understand which JavaScript module format is right for your project.. CSS is Global: Import vanduo.css once in your global entry file (e.g., main.js , _app.js , or layout.vue ).. JS Needs the DOM: Vanduo's JavaScript components (like modals, dropdowns, and tabs) need elements to exist in the DOM before initialization. You must call Vanduo.init() after your framework has mounted the component to the screen.. Never import Vanduo JS at the module top level in a server-rendered file. Use dynamic imports or guard with a browser check.. Always call Vanduo.init() inside a client-side lifecycle hook ( useEffect , onMounted , onMount ). These only run in the browser.. For Next.js App Router: mark the component that calls Vanduo.init() with 'use client'; at the top of the file.. Never import vanduo.css in individual components — it will duplicate styles and increase bundle size. One global import covers everything.. Re-initialization in SPAs: When dealing with Client-Side Routing (like next/router or vue-router ), navigating to a new page component replaces DOM elements. Ensure you call Vanduo.init() in the mounting phase of the new page component so Vanduo attaches its event listeners to the newly rendered elements!

chunks.text: Vanduo is designed to be completely framework-agnostic. Because it is built with pure CSS and Vanilla JavaScript modules, it can be dropped into any modern frontend framework (React, Vue, Nuxt, Svelte, etc.) seamlessly.
chunks.heading: Framework Integration Guide

chunks.text: When integrating Vanduo into a component-heavy framework, remember these two rules:
chunks.heading: The Core Concept

chunks.text: CSS is Global: Import vanduo.css once in your global entry file (e.g., main.js , _app.js , or layout.vue ).
chunks.heading: The Core Concept

chunks.text: JS Needs the DOM: Vanduo's JavaScript components (like modals, dropdowns, and tabs) need elements to exist in the DOM before initialization. You must call Vanduo.init() after your framework has mounted the component to the screen.
chunks.heading: The Core Concept

chunks.text: Vanduo's stylesheet should be imported once at the top level of your application. The exact file depends on your framework:
chunks.heading: Where to Import CSS

chunks.text: React (CRA) — src/index.js or src/App.js
chunks.heading: Where to Import CSS

chunks.heading: Where to Import CSS

chunks.text: Svelte / SvelteKit — src/routes/+layout.svelte or src/app.html
chunks.heading: Where to Import CSS

chunks.text: In a single-page application, navigating between routes replaces DOM elements without a full page reload. Vanduo's event listeners are attached to specific elements, so you need to re-initialize after each route change.
chunks.heading: Handling SPA Route Changes

chunks.text: The pattern is the same in every framework: call Vanduo.init() in the mounting lifecycle of each page component that uses Vanduo interactive features (modals, dropdowns, tabs, tooltips, etc.).
chunks.heading: Handling SPA Route Changes

chunks.text: Static content (grids, cards, typography, utilities) works without init() — only interactive JavaScript components need it.
chunks.heading: Handling SPA Route Changes

chunks.text: When using Server-Side Rendering (Next.js, Nuxt, SvelteKit), Vanduo's CSS works perfectly — it's just a stylesheet and renders on the server without issues.
chunks.heading: SSR & SSG Considerations

chunks.text: However, Vanduo's JavaScript requires the window and document APIs, which don't exist on the server. Follow these rules:
chunks.heading: SSR & SSG Considerations

chunks.text: Never import Vanduo JS at the module top level in a server-rendered file. Use dynamic imports or guard with a browser check.
chunks.heading: SSR & SSG Considerations

chunks.text: Always call Vanduo.init() inside a client-side lifecycle hook ( useEffect , onMounted , onMount ). These only run in the browser.
chunks.heading: SSR & SSG Considerations

chunks.text: For Next.js App Router: mark the component that calls Vanduo.init() with 'use client'; at the top of the file.
chunks.heading: SSR & SSG Considerations
